docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:115:+**M11 provider configuration.** Because rig.rs is required in M11 (see §K), the API key must be reachable in M11. In M11 the key is injected via environment variable (`NMP_LLM_API_KEY` / `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` / `NMP_LLM_BASE_URL`) at test time. The `KeyValueStoreCapability` bridge reads env vars at boot and writes them into the appropriate `podcast.llm.*` keys; `select_route()` reads `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route` which is itself populated from the KV store. When `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` is set, the bootstrap overwrites `llm_preferred_route` to `Rig { provider, model }` before the first action runs, so CI selects the rig route without any UI interaction. The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:149:    SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route=Rig before first action runs, so
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:368: The 8 KB transcript truncation cap and 12 KB chapter-extract cap from the Swift source are preserved as `const usize`. Future tuning is an ADR.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:379:             ↳ emit AskViewModule::Delta::TokenAppended (coalesced per ADR-0002)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:395: - **Per-session chat history**: in-memory `ChatSessionState`. Survives navigation. Drops 5 minutes after the session view module unsubscribes (per ADR-0005 warmth).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:396: - **Per-episode generated content** (summary, chapters, find-timestamp answers): persisted on `EpisodeRecord.ai_summary`, `ChapterRecord`s, and a `find_timestamp_cache: HashMap<(EpisodeId, query_hash), TimestampHit>` projection (size-capped at 100 entries per episode).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:397: - **Per-guest enrichment**: persisted on `GuestRecord.bio` + `GuestRecord.last_enriched_at_ms`. Stale after 30 days → silently re-enriched on next view open.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:411:+**M11 provider configuration.** Because rig.rs is required in M11 (see §K), the API key must be reachable in M11. In M11 the key is injected via environment variable (`NMP_LLM_API_KEY` / `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` / `NMP_LLM_BASE_URL`) at test time. The `KeyValueStoreCapability` bridge reads env vars at boot and writes them into the appropriate `podcast.llm.*` keys; `select_route()` reads `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route` which is itself populated from the KV store. When `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` is set, the bootstrap overwrites `llm_preferred_route` to `Rig { provider, model }` before the first action runs, so CI selects the rig route without any UI interaction. The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:491:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:392:Per ADR-0001, projection cache changes feed `ProjectionChange` events into `ViewModule::on_projection_changed`. Each is registered as a `ProjectionCache` in `nmp-core::substrate::projections` (kernel-owned trait; modules opt in).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:496:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:101:- **Recommendation**: (a) with snapshot writes. Future ADR.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:507:docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:82:Their target app has Wiki, Briefings, CarPlay, Watch, NIP-46, NIP-44, Cashu, peer-agent tool gates, App Intents/Siri, Live Activities — none of which exist in `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast`. Including them in M11 would defeat the kernel-boundary proof (the kernel-boundary check is only meaningful when the app stays small enough that the kernel could conceivably grow podcast nouns to make it easier).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:962:   263	**M11 provider configuration.** Because rig.rs is required in M11 (see §K), the API key must be reachable in M11. In M11 the key is injected via environment variable (`NMP_LLM_API_KEY` / `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` / `NMP_LLM_BASE_URL`) at test time. The `KeyValueStoreCapability` bridge reads env vars at boot and writes them into the appropriate `podcast.llm.*` keys; `select_route()` reads `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route` which is itself populated from the KV store. When `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` is set, the bootstrap overwrites `llm_preferred_route` to `Rig { provider, model }` before the first action runs, so CI selects the rig route without any UI interaction. The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.

docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:1:# ADR 0011: NMP owns the LMDB environment and injects it into nostr-lmdb
docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:6:**Depends on:** ADR-0009 (kernel boundary), ADR-0003 (working-set memory)
docs/decisions/0011-lmdb-env-sharing.md:68:This fallback is significantly more complex and has a measurable write-amplification cost. It is not the preferred design and should only be used if the upstream PR path is closed. The check `StoreHealth::consistency_status` (exposed to the diagnostics bridge per ADR-0007) surfaces any out-of-sync state detected at startup so it is always visible to developers.
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:18:1. **D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders** — the diagnostics lane (ADR-0007) wouldn't see the wire activity because it didn't go through the compiler.
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:76:4. **Bootstrap candidate discovery (untrusted; needs signer-identity + membership match)** — at session open, issue an indexer-style probe with filter `kinds: [39001, 39002], #p: [self_pubkey]` against the active relay pool. Each hit gives a *group_id candidate* (from the event's `d` tag) but **does not** identify the host relay (the relay that forwarded the event may be an indexer/cache, not the host; and per NIP-29 the same `local_id` may exist on multiple hosts as distinct groups). For each candidate group_id, the framework then re-issues *targeted* per-relay queries. A candidate relay `R` is accepted as the verified host of *the group the user is actually a member of* **iff all four hold**:
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:84:Without the membership check in (4), two hosts that reuse the same `local_id` would both pass the signer-identity check, and the cache would record whichever responded first — likely the wrong group. With the membership check, only the host whose membership snapshot actually contains the user can win, which is exactly the group the user is a member of.
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:86:Without the signer-identity match in (4), a general indexer (e.g. `purplepag.es`) that happens to forward a 39002 — or even an indexer that *also* forwards the host's 39000 verbatim — would be poisonously cached as the host, breaking subsequent host-pinned writes. The signer-identity match is what distinguishes "this relay produces 39000s for this group" from "this relay merely caches them".
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:92:Source (4) is the channel that prevents the silent-miss failure mode for users who become members via 9000 add or after device-restore. The probe in source (4) runs once at session open + on every `kind:0/3/10002` update for self, and dedups against the cache.
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:94:The cache is persisted (M3 LMDB), shared across app instances of the same account.
docs/design/nip29/routing.md:117:This means **no string-typed `h` tags pass through the planner without a `RelayPin` carrier** — the carrier is on the `PublishPlan` itself, set by the action that knows the pin. The planner consults the typed pin field for routing; **it does NOT inspect event tags to *derive* routing**. However — to prevent the privacy leak that would otherwise occur if a (possibly future, possibly third-party) action constructs an h-tagged event without setting `pin_to` — the planner DOES perform a single **defensive structural check** before dispatch: *if the event being published carries any `["h", _]` tag AND `pin_to` is `None`, the publish is rejected at construction time with a typed `MissingHostPinForGroupEvent` error*. The action must either set `pin_to` (correct) or strip the `h` tag (also correct; the event then routes normally). The test `nip29_publish_refuses_unpinned_h_tag_event` asserts this. This is the *only* tag-inspection the planner performs for NIP-29 — and it's a refusal, not a routing decision, so the "planner is crate-agnostic" property holds.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:40:`nmp-nip65` implements exactly one extension trait family: `ViewModule`. It does *not* implement `ActionModule` (kind:10002 publish is the user's own "update my relay list" action, deferred to M6's action ledger; in this milestone it has no write surface). It does *not* implement `DomainModule` (mailbox records live in the kernel-owned mailbox cache; they are queryable Nostr events, not durable app-defined records).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:218:- **No publish action.** Updating a user's own kind:10002 is `nmp-nip01::UpdateRelayList` (M6); that action depends on `nmp-nip65::parse` to validate the local copy before publishing.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:219:- **No outbox routing policy.** The decision "publish goes to author write relays + recipient inbox relays" is the publish planner ([outbox.md](outbox.md) §7), not this crate. This crate provides the lookups; the policy lives in `nmp-core::kernel::planner::publish`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:221:- **No FFI types.** `MailboxesPayload` is exposed at FFI via the per-app generated enum (per ADR-0010 codegen); the crate itself ships pure Rust.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:235:  `nmp-app-<name>` per ADR-0010.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:236:- Future `nmp-nip01::UpdateRelayList` (M6) — imports `parse::parse_relay_list` for validation.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/59f8d1b.md:12:session id: 019e3839-5a9d-7370-9c07-276d6ce6bf45
docs/perf/codex-reviews/59f8d1b.md:89:-**M11 provider configuration.** Because rig.rs is required in M11 (see §K), the API key must be reachable in M11. In M11 the key is injected via environment variable (`NMP_LLM_API_KEY` / `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` / `NMP_LLM_BASE_URL`) at test time. The `KeyValueStoreCapability` bridge reads env vars at boot and writes them into the appropriate `podcast.llm.*` keys; `select_route()` reads `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route` which is itself populated from the KV store. When `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` is set, the bootstrap overwrites `llm_preferred_route` to `Rig { provider, model }` before the first action runs, so CI selects the rig route without any UI interaction. The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.

The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.

In M11 the key is injected via environment variable (`NMP_LLM_API_KEY` / `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` / `NMP_LLM_BASE_URL`) at test time. The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:1:# ADR 0006: Vertical-slice-first delivery for Phase 1
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:4:**Status:** accepted (discipline preserved; positioning modified by ADR-0009)
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:5:**Modified by:** ADR-0009 (the slice is now built on top of the kernel substrate; the kind:0 path is a Profile `ViewModule` in `nmp-nip01`, not a built-in feature of `nmp-core`)
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:9:The firehose-bench harness runs in three modes (replay, capture, live). Replay and capture currently model the runtime; live correctly reports `blocked` because the real adapters do not exist. Reactivity-bench run 002 validated the reactive model against ADRs 0001–0005 in a self-consistent fashion.
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:31:│  Generated wrapper (ADR-0005, manually written for slice)   │
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:50:│  - kind:0 replaceable supersession                           │
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:71:- **Multi-account.** Single account, single signer. Account scope is in the API from the start but not exercised yet.
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:92:- The `OpenView`/`CloseView` API shape is right (or surfaces the bugs that need ADR follow-ups).
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:104:- Multi-account isolation (Phase 3).

docs/design/view-catalog/template-and-enumeration.md:11:> **Prerequisites:** `product-spec.md` §7.6, `reactivity.md`, `kernel-substrate.md` §3, ADR-0005, ADR-0010.

docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:1:# M6 — Sessions + signers + write path
docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:11:- `IdentityModule::HumanAccount` with local-key signer (raw nsec, NIP-49 encrypted).
docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:12:- `IdentityModule::ExternalSigner` with NIP-46 (Nostr Connect / bunker) signer.
docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:13:- **NIP-46 `bunker://` URL parsing + connection flow**: a pasted `bunker://` URL is a first-class signer onboarding path; the framework wires the rendezvous (per 2026-05-18 scope adjustments).
docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:14:- **Create new nsec flow**: generate, encrypt (NIP-49), and store via `KeychainCapability`. User-initiated through a single action; no app needs to roll its own (per 2026-05-18 scope adjustments).
docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:15:- `KeychainCapability` real implementation: encrypted nsec storage via iOS Keychain, app-private access group.
docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:19:- Login UX: paste nsec, paste `bunker://`, or generate new nsec — all three flows in M6.
docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:24:- Bug-extinction #9 (NIP-46 lost on suspend): simulate suspend mid-publish; resume retries or surfaces failure as toast.
docs/decisions/0003-working-set-memory.md:1:# ADR 0003: Memory budget is for working set, not total cached events

docs/plan/m8-subscription-lifecycle.md:7:> session-scope concern; this is a connection-pool / trigger-fan-in concern.

docs/plan/m8-subscription-lifecycle.md:45:| Active-account scope binding (rebuild on switch) | No | M8 multi-account |

docs/plan/m8-subscription-lifecycle.md:68:  opaque `AccountId` / `SignerId` type aliases.
docs/plan/m8-subscription-lifecycle.md:157:  tick (≤60 Hz/view budget per ADR-0002). The wire-emitter's diff allocates

docs/plan/m8-subscription-lifecycle.md:169:  `Trigger::ActiveAccountChanged` variant; the session-state machine wires it.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:4:> Read first: ADR-0007 (`docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md`) — every record here extends ADR-0007 types; it does not replace them.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:6:The compiler's routing decisions are the most subtle correctness surface in the M2 milestone. They are also the easiest to silently get wrong (`docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §3, "automatic behaviour also needs strong tests"). Diagnostics make the four sources of relay knowledge legible — separately, never collapsed.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:42:Each lane has one record type. All four are exposed to the platform via the existing ADR-0007 `ViewBatch` lane (low-cadence, coalesced to 1–4 Hz per ADR-0007 "How status crosses the bridge"). They feed into the diagnostics screen, not into normal product UI.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:106:Emitted by `handle_event` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:134-164`) for every EVENT arrival. This is the highest-cardinality lane and the only one where coalescing matters at the ADR-0007 boundary: the platform diagnostic view consumes a summarised projection (`ProvenanceSummary` per author or per event), not the raw fact stream.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:135:The compiler may *use* facts from multiple lanes to compute a routing decision; it may **not** present them as one. Concretely:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:137:- **Routing decision** (`AuthorRouting.source` from [compiler.md](compiler.md) §3.1): records *which lane* the relay set was derived from. Single-valued; one of `Nip65 | Hint | UserConfigured`. There is no `Indexer` variant — indexer fallback routing is `UserConfigured` with the relay's `UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer` sub-category visible in the `UserConfiguredRelayFact` record. The author may have facts in three lanes; the compiler picks one, says so, and the other lane records remain visible.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:149:- `User-configured` is the **only** lane the compiler treats as authoritative-by-policy (the user said so). Open question 5 in the parent index resolves the augment-vs-override precedence between NIP-65 and user-configured for the active account.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:151:The lesson the four-lane discipline preserves: routing is **derivable but contested** evidence. Collapsing the lanes loses information; preserving them lets the diagnostic answer "why did we route this REQ to that relay?" months after the decision.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:202:3. `UserConfiguredRelayFact` records — increments/decrements `by_lane.user_configured` per (relay_url) membership. Records with `category == Indexer` contribute to `by_lane.indexer_fallback` (a sub-count of `user_configured`); the UI can show both the aggregate and the Indexer-specific sub-total.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:212:Emission cadence follows ADR-0007's diagnostic-view rule: material-transition immediately, otherwise 1–4 Hz. The provenance counter ticks every second; the `by_lane` counts only emit on `CompiledPlan` recompiles or new mailbox arrivals.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:216:Per `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("the developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does") and ADR-0007's domain-of-diagnostics separation, end-user product UIs do not show "relay X is serving 12 authors." That is operator/debug surface. Normal apps consume the `LogicalInterestStatus` summaries; `RelayCoveragePayload` is for the diagnostics screen (proof iOS app screenshot in `docs/perf/m2/outbox-routing.md` per [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)).
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:1:# ADR 0007: Diagnostics and non-Nostr domain data over the app bridge
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:8:ADR-0005 defines the platform shadow: components read from typed, domain-keyed platform caches while Rust owns source-of-truth state and emits bounded deltas. ADR-0006 narrows the first runtime proof to one profile metadata vertical slice.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:177:The ADR-0006 profile slice implements the smallest useful subset:
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:194:| Action ledger | Status of side effects and user intents | publish attempts, uploads, downloads, NWC requests, signer prompts | `AppState` summary + action/status views |
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:205:- NIP-46 pairing URI is ephemeral until accepted; it crosses as `SideEffect::BunkerPairingReady`.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:215:- Wallet and media views key by wallet id, upload id, asset id, or account pubkey.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:237:- The ADR-0006 desktop slice exposes relay status, one profile logical interest, and the backing wire subscription.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:1:# ADR 0008: Twitter-clone on iOS as the Phase 1a demo target
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:4:**Status:** accepted (positioning modified by ADR-0009)
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:5:**Supersedes (in part):** ADR-0006 (in choice of demo target only — discipline preserved)
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:6:**Relates to:** ADR-0005 (domain-keyed shadow), ADR-0007 (diagnostics bridge)
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:7:**Modified by:** ADR-0009 — the Twitter clone is now built as **the first canonical extension module set** (`twitter-core` app crate on top of `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / `nmp-nip10` / `nmp-nip25` protocol modules), not as a built-in feature of `nmp-core`. Sub-phase plan adjusted: 1a.0 (foundations) is followed by 1a.1 (**kernel substrate + non-Nostr fixture module**) BEFORE 1a.2 (first Nostr protocol module + desktop avatar slice). Twitter clone features layer on from 1a.4 onward.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:11:ADR-0006 chose a deliberately narrow demo target — kind:0 profile metadata on desktop iced — as the Phase 1a vertical slice. The point was risk reduction: prove the architecture works in code with the fewest variables, no FFI, one view kind, read-only.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:15:The right way to manage the added scope is to keep ADR-0006's walking-skeleton discipline but make each step bigger: six sub-phases, each a walking skeleton in its own right, each adding exactly one architectural ingredient on top of working code.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:32:**Seed-driven timeline as the default read mode.** The unauthenticated timeline is not "this user's follows" (there is no user yet); it's the **union of follows of a small set of hardcoded seed dev accounts**. The app opens `Contacts` views for each seed, unions their follow lists into an `authors` set (typically several hundred to a few thousand pubkeys), and opens a `Timeline` view with that author set. This gives the demo real breadth from the first launch — fresh content from across the Nostr social graph, not a contrived test feed. It also stress-tests the framework on a realistic author-set size from sub-phase 1a.3 onward.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:34:ADR-0006's discipline survives intact: walking skeleton, one ingredient per sub-phase, runtime evidence over modeled budgets, harness gates on every layer. ADR-0006's specific recommendation (desktop iced as the slice) is replaced by **iOS as the demo target, desktop iced as a non-shipping diagnostic reference target alongside.**
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:36:## Slice-blocker decisions
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:43:| Profile slice test pubkey (1a.1–1a.2) | TBD — recommend the repo owner (pablof7z) or fiatjaf for a known-stable kind:0. | Stable kind:0 needed to exercise the placeholder→refined transition on a real account. |
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:44:| Seed dev accounts for timeline discovery (1a.3+) | TBD — suggested starter set: fiatjaf, jb55, pablof7z. ~3 seeds gives several hundred to a few thousand followed authors via union. | Their follow lists union into the demo timeline's author set. Real breadth, fresh content, no contrived feed, realistic author-set size for the planner. |
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:66:The original ADR-0006 slice. Desktop iced binary; in-memory `EventStore` with kind:0 supersession + composite reverse index `(kind, author)`; minimal `Profile` view kind; manually-written `useProfile(pubkey)` wrapper for iced; one WebSocket via `nostr-sdk` to primal.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:68:Includes ADR-0007 diagnostics minimum: one `RelayStatus` row, one `LogicalInterestStatus::Profile { pubkey }`, one `WireSubscriptionStatus` for the kind:0 REQ.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:77:- iOS app shell: XcodeGen `project.yml`, SwiftUI entry point, `AppManager` (`@Observable`) implementing `AppReconciler`, `KeychainCapability` minimal (placeholder; full credential storage in 1a.4).
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:79:- iOS-specific `useProfile` wrapper using `@Observable` + property-wrapper pattern, refcounted per pubkey per ADR-0005.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:88:- Contacts view kind (parsed kind:3 follow list) per `view-catalog.md` §9. **Multiple Contacts views open simultaneously — one per seed dev account.** Their follow lists union into the timeline's author set.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:94:**Exit gate.** Cold launch with primed LMDB renders the seed-driven timeline in ≤ 1.5s, showing fresh content from hundreds of authors followed by the seeds; tap an author → profile screen → back works; kind:0 arriving mid-scroll updates all author rows in place per doctrine D1; updating one seed's kind:3 mid-session re-resolves the timeline's author set without manual intervention; reactivity-bench `--standard` continues to pass at the larger author-set size; firehose-bench `live` for `sustained_firehose` (running at the real seed-author scale, not modeled) lands within budgets.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:96:### 1a.4 — Login + Signer + Compose (~1.5 weeks)
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:100:- Signer trait shape with local-key implementation (raw nsec).
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:101:- `ActionCx` with event_store, signer, publisher, active_account fields.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:104:- `KeychainCapability` real implementation: encrypted nsec storage via iOS Keychain (app-private access group for now; shared group for NSE in Phase 5).
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:107:- **Timeline source switch.** Logged-in users get an optional "Home" mode that uses the active account's own kind:3 follow list as the timeline's author set, replacing the seed-driven set. UI toggle between "Discover" (seed-driven) and "Home" (your follows). Unauthenticated users only see "Discover."
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:131:- Diagnostics screen: opens `ViewSpec::NetworkDiagnostics` per ADR-0007; shows RelayStatus + active LogicalInterestStatus + WireSubscriptionStatus.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:144:| 1a.4 | **Signer trait shape + ActionCx fields** (currently sketched). **Login UX flow** (single screen vs onboarding wizard). **KeychainCapability iOS access groups** (app-private for now, named group placeholder for NSE). Pre-1a.4 deliverable. |
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:146:| 1a.6 | Pagination cursor semantics under primal's real EOSE behavior; diagnostics screen layout (read-only renderer over ADR-0007 records). |
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:168:- **Phase 1b becomes "expand to multi-relay + outbox + full view kinds + other platforms"** rather than "expand to anything beyond Profile." Some Phase 2 (sync engine, negentropy) and Phase 3 (multi-account, more signer kinds) work also reasonably moves earlier.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:170:- **Diagnostics from ADR-0007 are first-class** in 1a.1 onward, not bolted on later.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:175:- **Keep ADR-0006 as-is (desktop avatar only).** Rejected — the demo doesn't show the framework's actual value proposition.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:186:- `firehose-bench live` runs more scenarios with measured numbers as the adapters expand (1a.1: cold_start + profile_thrashing slice; 1a.3: + sustained_firehose; 1a.4: + multi_account stub; 1a.6: most of the scenario set).
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:1:# ADR 0010: Generated per-app concrete enums at the FFI boundary
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:6:**Depends on:** ADR-0009 (kernel boundary)
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:10:ADR-0009 establishes that NMP is a kernel with extension modules. Apps assemble themselves from a kernel (`nmp-core`), a set of protocol modules (`nmp-nip01`, `nmp-nip17`, ...), and an app-specific core crate (`twitter-core`, `highlighter-core`, ...). Each layer can define typed `AppAction` variants, `AppUpdate` variants, `ViewSpec` variants, and so on.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:17:This decision affects every layer of the framework.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:184:- Phase 1a.1 (kernel substrate prototype) generates a tiny `nmp-app-fixture` crate with one module set demonstrating DomainModule, ViewModule, ActionModule, CapabilityModule, and IdentityModule. Bindings compile and round-trip a dispatch in the reference shell.
docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:19:Parsed kind:10000 for the active account. Payload: `MutesView { muted_pubkeys: Vec<PubKey>, muted_hashtags: Vec<String>, muted_events: Vec<EventId>, muted_words: Vec<String> }`. Affects projection of "should this event be filtered out of timeline?" Phase 2.
docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:31:List of DM conversations for the active account, with last-message preview + unread count. Payload: `ConversationListView { conversations: Vec<ConversationSummary>, total_unread: u32 }`. Phase 5.
docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:35:Bidirectional list of zaps sent/received for the active account. Payload: `ZapHistoryView { sent: Vec<ZapEntry>, received: Vec<ZapEntry>, cursor }`. Phase 6.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:5:> **Companion docs:** [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md); `docs/aim.md` §4.4–§4.5; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7; ADR-0007 (diagnostic lanes); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) and §4 (`ActionModule`); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2–§7.3.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:25:| 4 | Recompilation triggers (kind:10002, view open/close, reconnect, account switch, manual, user-configured change) | recompilation.md |
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:31:| 10 | Open questions for follow-up ADRs | this file (below) |
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:35:These remain to be resolved by ADRs after design review, not in this design pass.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:42:2. **Filter-merge lattice formal-isation.** §3 step 3 lists which filter fields are safely mergeable across logical interests (`authors`, `kinds`, `since`, `until`, `limit`, tag operators). It does not yet specify the merge algebra for the corner case where two interests differ only in `limit` but agree on authors and kinds. Worth an ADR-level note before the lattice is coded; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2 promises "a formal merge lattice for `limit`, `since`, `until`, multi-filter arrays, and tag operators."
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:43:3. **Per-author indexer-fallback ledger row?** Today the compiler treats indexer fallback as an inline relay assignment. If the kind:10002 fetch is its own durable action (M6 ledger), the fallback becomes a tracked action with retry/cancel semantics. Cleaner for diagnostics; heavier for M2. Defer.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:44:4. **Read-relay vs write-relay use for subscriptions on the same author.** NIP-65 defines write relays (outbox) for the author's own events and read relays (inbox) for events directed *at* the author. For a `Timeline { authors: [...] }` we want write relays. For a `Notifications { p: [author] }` we want inbox relays. The compiler distinguishes them by filter shape (`authors` vs `#p`), matching the `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 routing table. Document a corner case: kind-1 filtered by both `authors` and `#p` is rare but real (replies to the author from the author). Pick a precedence in an ADR; current bias is `authors` wins (write relays).
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:45:5. **User-configured relay precedence vs NIP-65.** A user adds `wss://my-private.example` to local config. Does it *augment* (union) or *override* (replace) NIP-65 routing for the active account? `subsystems.md` §7.3 default-resolves by NIP-65; user-configured is "fallback" in the indexer sense. ADR needs to spell out the augment/override question for the active account specifically.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:47:7. **NSE crate compilation surface.** `nmp-nip17-nse` (M9) runs in iOS Notification Service Extension with bounded memory; it needs to compile a single-author single-relay plan without the full planner. Confirm in an ADR that the compiler exposes a `compile_one(spec, mailbox_cache_snapshot) -> Plan` pure function suitable for NSE use, and that the function does not require a live actor.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:48:8. **Relay-count optimization (greedy set-cover).** Stage 3 currently assigns interests to every declared write relay. Applesauce's `selectOptimalRelays` (`docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md` §3, `relay-selection.ts:14-93`) shows a greedy set-cover heuristic that caps to `maxConnections` relays while maximizing author coverage. NMP should add an equivalent cap policy in a future ADR, after M2 proves the basic per-relay fan-out is correct. Referenced by `compiler.md` §3.3.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:24:| A4 | session | `Trigger::ActiveAccountChanged { from, to }` | account switch (M8) |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:30:| A10 | session | `Trigger::SignerAvailable { account, signer_id }` | signer-loss-then-recovery (M6+) |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:71:        state: RelayAuthState,              // re-exported from ADR-0007
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:73:    SignerAvailable {
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:74:        account: AccountId,
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:75:        signer_id: SignerId,
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:97:Outbox routing implication: if `pubkey` was previously routed to the indexer fallback set (Stage 2 read-fallback), the compiler now reassigns to the author's declared write relays. The wire-emitter closes the indexer REQ for that author's slice and opens a new REQ on the declared relays. ADR-0007 diagnostics reflect the route source flipping from `UserConfigured::Indexer` to `Nip65`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:105:Batching contract: the actor's planner inbox coalesces consecutive `ViewOpened` triggers within one actor tick into a single recompile pass. This is the existing reactivity batching (`docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md`) extended to the planner; the M2 implementation respects the same `≤60Hz/view` budget from ADR-0002 by capping recompiles at one per tick regardless of trigger fan-in.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:109:Emitted by the view registry after the warmth grace expires for an interest with refcount = 0. The warmth window is configurable (`AppConfig.view_warmth_ms`, default 30,000 — matching the doctrine in `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 "View warmth"). Closing an interest mid-warmth (e.g. account switch invalidates the prior account's interests) is a separate `ActiveAccountChanged` trigger, not this one.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:113:M2 establishes the trigger; M8 wires the multi-account state machine that actually emits it. For M2, the trigger fires once at startup with `from: None, to: Some(active)` so the test surface can exercise account-scope binding without waiting for M8.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:141:### A10 — SignerAvailable
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:143:M6+ trigger. Some interests (private DMs in M9, NIP-42 challenge response in M5) only become routable once a signer is loaded for their account. M2 records the trigger shape; behaviour is no-op pre-M6.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:145:Implementation note: **signer must be bound before emitting `SignerAvailable`** — NDK gotcha
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:147:binding the signer causes reactive consumers to run with no-signer or stale-signer. NMP's
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:148:actor must bind the signer to the `SessionState` synchronously before emitting this trigger.
docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:7:**Scope.** Replace the current raw C FFI surface in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs` with the per-app generated `nmp-app-<name>` crate per ADR-0010. The iOS app stops importing `NmpCore.h` and instead imports the generated Swift module.
docs/plan/m14-uniffi.md:22:- Platform debounce dispatch-rate gate (deferred from M1 per T22): with the M14 generated wrapper (ADR-0005 refcounted component wrapper) in place, validate that mount/unmount churn at 1000/sec for 60 s produces dispatch rate (OpenView + CloseView combined) ≤ 60% of mount rate. The grace-period absorption (30 s by default) is what makes this gate green; without it the wrapper is doing pass-through and the dedup belongs purely to the kernel.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:12:- **The seam that emits REQs is parameterized by `RelayRole`.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:530-556` (`req()`) inserts a `WireSub { role, .. }` keyed by a string sub-id and emits `OutboundMessage { role, text }`. The role *is* the routing decision; there is no relay-URL field on `WireSub` or `OutboundMessage`. Any compiler that fans an interest out across N URLs has to replace this helper.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:17:- **No publish path exists yet.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:30` (no occurrences of `EVENT` outbound) and `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:42-45` (`OutboundMessage` carries only role + text). The first publish action (M6 `SendNote`) will hit this same `req()`-style seam. M2 must establish the planner shape before M6 builds the first user of it; the doctrine "no developer-supplied relays for a publish" (`docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 row "Publish leaked to wrong relays") needs a structural enforcement point.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:19:The summary diagnosis: **the planner is a string formatter, not a compiler.** Every REQ is a per-call-site decision; routing is one of two literals; recompilation is impossible because nothing is compiled. The diagnostics in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:117-154` already type `RelayStatus` / `WireSubscriptionStatus` / `LogicalInterestStatus` per ADR-0007 — but the planner currently emits at most one `LogicalInterestStatus` per view kind because there is no logical-interest object to scope it against.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:41:    /// Account-scoped interests resolve via the active account's mailbox view
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:60:    /// Bound to the active account in SessionState. Re-routes on account switch.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:62:    /// Bound to a specific account (multi-account UIs, M8). Re-routes on that
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:63:    /// account's mailbox refresh; ignored on account switch.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:65:    /// No account context. Used for global pointer loaders, NIP-19-driven
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:129:    /// recompilation triggers that the view module opts into (account-switch,
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:136:`InterestContext` exposes read access to the mailbox cache and to the active account but **not** to the relay set; the view module never names relays. That keeps `nmp-aim.md` doctrine 5 ("outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") structurally enforced — there is no place for a view module to write a URL.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:167:The kernel `SessionState` (`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.4; M8 graduates this to multi-account) carries an active account id. `InterestScope::ActiveAccount` resolves at compile time, not at registration time. On account switch (§4 trigger A4), the compiler re-evaluates every `ActiveAccount`-scoped interest against the new active account's mailbox view. This is the structural enforcement of bug-extinction #5 (account-context overlap) referenced in [`docs/plan/m8-multi-account.md`](../../plan/m8-multi-account.md) — the kernel cannot "forget" to re-route because every plan re-derives from the active scope.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:169:Account-scoped interests with empty `authors` and empty `#p` (e.g. a free-form hashtag firehose) resolve against the active account's *read relays* (NIP-65 read side) — the user's own subscription preferences, not a globally hardcoded relay. Today's `firehose_requests()` at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:357-372` hardcodes `RelayRole::Content`; under the compiler this becomes "active-account read relays, falling back to indexer set if the active account has no kind:10002."
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:175:- A **wire REQ**. Wire REQs are produced by the compiler; they live in `WireSubscriptionStatus` per ADR-0007.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:177:- A **diagnostic record**. ADR-0007 lanes are facts derived from the planner's state, not inputs.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:950:827:- rollout_summaries/2026-05-17T17-15-09-MoTM-nmp_stress_test_simulator_and_scroll_perf.md (cwd=/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform, rollout_path=/Users/pablofernandez/.codex/sessions/2026/05/17/rollout-2026-05-17T20-15-09-019e36ef-3ed6-7aa0-9faf-26c68cb99652.jsonl, updated_at=2026-05-17T17:54:30+00:00, thread_id=019e36ef-3ed6-7aa0-9faf-26c68cb99652, partial, structural success but large-row scroll path not yet proven performant)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:1026:> **Status:** Draft 0, revised for ADR-0009 and ADR-0010. The kernel/module split is now architectural ground truth; product modules still graduate by the phased plan in [`docs/plan.md`](../plan.md).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:1036:The kernel composes the `rust-nostr` crate family plus OS capability crates into a substrate. It owns actor runtime, verified event store, subscription planner, relay routing pipeline, signer/session plumbing, durable action ledger, domain-store substrate, typed view registry, capability bridge, platform shadow/codegen machinery, diagnostics, and test harnesses.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:1040:The framework treats common Nostr-correctness failures (stale replaceable events, lost subscriptions, mis-routed publishes, double-publication, multi-account desync, leaked secrets across FFI, naive cache invalidation, withheld cached data, blocking-on-fetch UI patterns) as **product defects in the framework** rather than as developer mistakes. The public API is designed so that the wrong thing is hard to type.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:1048:Summary: D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime implementation constraints). Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:1104:Result on each platform: a starter app with login (private key + NIP-46 bunker), a "following" timeline, compose, profile view, profile edit, and a DM inbox + thread. End-to-end build + first launch ≤ 5 minutes on a developer laptop with the framework's `nix develop` shell active, ≤ 15 minutes from cold without Nix.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:1127:5. Two account contexts having overlapping mutable state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:1131:9. NIP-46 signing session lost on app suspend/resume without prompt.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:1193:`examples/chat-{ios,android,desktop,web}` track the starter app but include richer features (groups via NIP-29, zaps end-to-end, Blossom uploads, NIP-46 bunker pairing) and serve as the canonical "what does production-grade integration look like" reference for each platform.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:3006:    1265 docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:3047:     5	Nine named principles that subsume the rest of the spec. Every API decision answers to at least one of these; conflicts between them resolve in the order listed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:3053:    11	Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:3111:    69	- Silent failure: every error has at least one observable state field carrying its consequences (a toast, a `busy` flag clearing, a diagnostic record per ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:3117:    75	Native bridges (Keychain, NIP-46 bunker, BGTask scheduler, `AVPlayer`, NIP-07 web extension, FilePicker, Blossom upload, etc.) execute platform APIs and report raw events back into the kernel. They never decide *whether to retry*, *whether an error is recoverable*, *which relay to publish to*, *which encryption scheme to negotiate*, *what state should become*, or *whether a duplicate request is a no-op*. Policy is Rust's; capabilities are reports.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:3121:    79	- Capability bridges holding cached state beyond transient OS handles (Keychain handle, audio session token, network monitor, push registration).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:3122:    80	- Native code making decisions that the kernel should reproduce identically across platforms.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:3140:    98	Per ADRs 0001 (composite dependency keys), 0002 (per-view delta budget), 0003 (working-set memory), and 0004 (allocation measurement), all in `docs/decisions/`. Validated continuously by `reactivity-bench` (`crates/nmp-testing/bin/reactivity-bench/`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:3864:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:3865:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:20:| 2026-05-18 05:05 | 10 | Heartbeat #10 (autonomous). MAJOR WAVE COMPLETE: T18 highlighter-explorer wrapped after 14 codex iterations (16 commits cherry-picked from orphan branch; T18's proposed ADRs 0011/0012 renumbered to 0012/0013 to avoid clash with T14's ADR-0011 LMDB env sharing; ffi-hardening's stale "ADR-0011 for M14" reference fixed to point to a new ADR-0014); README updated to mark M11.5 design landed. T25 m3-codex-followup wrapped CLEAN — pushed correctly to master via HEAD:master refspec (the new protocol mitigation working as designed). All 6 codex findings from f765f60 addressed: HIGH scan-ordering fixed via new idx_kind_dtag_time index; MED WriteCounting redesigned as backend test hook (LmdbEventStore::with_write_observer behind test-support feature); MED BTreeSet claim idempotency; MED D3→D6 cite fix; LOW coverage() signature aligned; LOW tests.md split into mod+insert.md+migration.md. All design surfaces (M2/M3/M10.5/M11/M11.5/framework-magic) now codex-clean. Queued + dispatched IMPLEMENTATION phase: T27 (M3 EventStore trait + MemEventStore + LmdbEventStore skeleton in new crates/nmp-store) + T28 (M10.5 ffi-stress harness phase 1, scenarios S1-S5). Held T26 (M2 compiler impl) for next heartbeat to serialize against T27's ingest swap. 2 agents in flight; first impl-phase agents this session. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:3868:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:29:| 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:4598:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:336:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:20:| 2026-05-18 05:05 | 10 | Heartbeat #10 (autonomous). MAJOR WAVE COMPLETE: T18 highlighter-explorer wrapped after 14 codex iterations (16 commits cherry-picked from orphan branch; T18's proposed ADRs 0011/0012 renumbered to 0012/0013 to avoid clash with T14's ADR-0011 LMDB env sharing; ffi-hardening's stale "ADR-0011 for M14" reference fixed to point to a new ADR-0014); README updated to mark M11.5 design landed. T25 m3-codex-followup wrapped CLEAN — pushed correctly to master via HEAD:master refspec (the new protocol mitigation working as designed). All 6 codex findings from f765f60 addressed: HIGH scan-ordering fixed via new idx_kind_dtag_time index; MED WriteCounting redesigned as backend test hook (LmdbEventStore::with_write_observer behind test-support feature); MED BTreeSet claim idempotency; MED D3→D6 cite fix; LOW coverage() signature aligned; LOW tests.md split into mod+insert.md+migration.md. All design surfaces (M2/M3/M10.5/M11/M11.5/framework-magic) now codex-clean. Queued + dispatched IMPLEMENTATION phase: T27 (M3 EventStore trait + MemEventStore + LmdbEventStore skeleton in new crates/nmp-store) + T28 (M10.5 ffi-stress harness phase 1, scenarios S1-S5). Held T26 (M2 compiler impl) for next heartbeat to serialize against T27's ingest swap. 2 agents in flight; first impl-phase agents this session. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:4601:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:341:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:29:| 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:4721:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:852:docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md:37:The `profile_thrashing` and `mount_unmount_churn` scenarios note "process RSS ≤ baseline + N MB" as assertions, but M1 has no OS-level instrumentation to measure them (requires `mach_task_basic_info` on Apple, `/proc/<pid>/status` on Linux). M10.5 is the dedicated FFI-hardening + iPhone-12 measurement pass; it adds scenario S11 to capture RSS via `mach_task_basic_info` during the canonical workflow on real hardware. The numeric gate (≤ 100 MB simulator / ≤ 150 MB iPhone 12 at 100 active views per ADR-0003 working-set gate) lives in M10.5 S11 / gates.md §G-S11.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:4744:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:1123:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e8b1726.md:19:- `gates.md`: added G-S11 numeric exit gate table (100 MiB sim / 150 MiB iPhone 12, consistent with ADR-0003).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:4751:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:1343:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:820:-        .with_note("G-S1/harness: RSS growth <= 10 MiB (2× spec; accounts for harness emit rate)"),
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:4799:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:1435:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2271: | 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:5512:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:8028:2271: | 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:6257:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2271: | 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:6588:docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:1914:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:20:| 2026-05-18 05:05 | 10 | Heartbeat #10 (autonomous). MAJOR WAVE COMPLETE: T18 highlighter-explorer wrapped after 14 codex iterations (16 commits cherry-picked from orphan branch; T18's proposed ADRs 0011/0012 renumbered to 0012/0013 to avoid clash with T14's ADR-0011 LMDB env sharing; ffi-hardening's stale "ADR-0011 for M14" reference fixed to point to a new ADR-0014); README updated to mark M11.5 design landed. T25 m3-codex-followup wrapped CLEAN — pushed correctly to master via HEAD:master refspec (the new protocol mitigation working as designed). All 6 codex findings from f765f60 addressed: HIGH scan-ordering fixed via new idx_kind_dtag_time index; MED WriteCounting redesigned as backend test hook (LmdbEventStore::with_write_observer behind test-support feature); MED BTreeSet claim idempotency; MED D3→D6 cite fix; LOW coverage() signature aligned; LOW tests.md split into mod+insert.md+migration.md. All design surfaces (M2/M3/M10.5/M11/M11.5/framework-magic) now codex-clean. Queued + dispatched IMPLEMENTATION phase: T27 (M3 EventStore trait + MemEventStore + LmdbEventStore skeleton in new crates/nmp-store) + T28 (M10.5 ffi-stress harness phase 1, scenarios S1-S5). Held T26 (M2 compiler impl) for next heartbeat to serialize against T27's ingest swap. 2 agents in flight; first impl-phase agents this session. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:6589:docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2273:docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md:37:The `profile_thrashing` and `mount_unmount_churn` scenarios note "process RSS ≤ baseline + N MB" as assertions, but M1 has no OS-level instrumentation to measure them (requires `mach_task_basic_info` on Apple, `/proc/<pid>/status` on Linux). M10.5 is the dedicated FFI-hardening + iPhone-12 measurement pass; it adds scenario S11 to capture RSS via `mach_task_basic_info` during the canonical workflow on real hardware. The numeric gate (≤ 100 MB simulator / ≤ 150 MB iPhone 12 at 100 active views per ADR-0003 working-set gate) lives in M10.5 S11 / gates.md §G-S11.

All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:6901:docs/perf/codex-reviews/deb93fc.md:97:+//! snapshot-based heap accounting used by S1, S2, S3, S5 to detect
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:6932:docs/perf/codex-reviews/deb93fc.md:946:+        .with_note("G-S1/harness: RSS growth <= 10 MiB (2× spec; accounts for harness emit rate)"),
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:7055:docs/perf/codex-reviews/deb93fc.md:3258:   145	        .with_note("G-S1/harness: RSS growth <= 10 MiB (2× spec; accounts for harness emit rate)"),
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:7117:docs/perf/codex-reviews/deb93fc.md:4446:     4	//! snapshot-based heap accounting used by S1, S2, S3, S5 to detect
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:7146:docs/perf/codex-reviews/deb93fc.md:5965:- [crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:201): S4 hardcodes `dropped_emits == 0` and uses emit-count delta as backlog. It does not check actor queue depth, stale-rev drops, or apply-after-resume burst. Fix with actual expected-vs-delivered emit accounting and iOS/XCUITest-side metrics.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:7155:docs/perf/codex-reviews/deb93fc.md:5994:- [crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:201): S4 hardcodes `dropped_emits == 0` and uses emit-count delta as backlog. It does not check actor queue depth, stale-rev drops, or apply-after-resume burst. Fix with actual expected-vs-delivered emit accounting and iOS/XCUITest-side metrics.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:7587:docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:744:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:2078:docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:4995:docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:1896:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1847:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6544:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:2095:docs/design/nip29-crate.md:184:5. **Invite-code redemption UX vs JoinRequest.** The 9021 with a `code` tag is the redemption path. The current Highlighter onboarding lets the user paste a code before any signer is installed. Does the redemption action defer until a signer exists, or do we mint a fresh local key and redeem immediately? Cross-cuts M6 (signer flows). ADR-level question.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:2199:docs/research/applesauce/gotchas.md:52:**Reading `signers/amber-clipboard-signer.ts:65-68`** (no specific commit, design observation). `visibilitychange` fires as soon as `window.open` is called. A naive `pendingRequest = createDefer()` immediately before the intent open would resolve with stale clipboard content. The workaround is a 500ms setTimeout before installing the pending request.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:2319:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:15:**Framework does:** the subscription-compilation pipeline (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md`) for dedup and coalesce; the wire-emitter's diff (compiler §3 final stage) for auto-close on plan changes; the view registry's refcount drop for auto-close on consumer loss; `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` for the per-tick batching; the FullState backpressure fallback at `subsystems.md` §7.2 line 69. The ≤60Hz cap is D8 (reactivity contract per `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D8; budget validated by ADR-0002 in `docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md` §7.2).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:2421:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2271: | 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:2448:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:4630:   253	- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:2526:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:2813:docs/perf/codex-reviews/85770db.md:2769:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2418:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1847:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6544:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3128:docs/perf/codex-reviews/85770db.md:3590:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5543:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2070:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3280:docs/perf/codex-reviews/85770db.md:4023:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:3029:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:985:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2070:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3444:docs/perf/codex-reviews/85770db.md:4702:docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2542:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2140:/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"app nouns|substrate|kernel|D0|D1|D2|D3|D4|D5|best-effort|negentropy|outbox|manual relay|single writer|bounded|M9|M11|M11\\.5|M12|Wallet|DM|podcast|Highlighter|defer|deferred|scope\" docs/aim.md docs/plan.md docs/product-spec.md docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md docs/design/framework-magic.md docs/design/framework-magic docs/design/subscription-compilation.md docs/design/subscription-compilation docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md docs/design/kernel-substrate.md docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada
docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3451:docs/perf/codex-reviews/85770db.md:4730:docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2717:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2571:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2001:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8243:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:578:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3462:docs/perf/codex-reviews/85770db.md:4800:docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3062:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2918:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1456:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1698:/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "D0|D1|D2|D3|D4|D5|Doctrine|kernel never grows|single writer|capabilities report|M2|subscription compiler|subscription compilation|outbox|NIP-65|watermark|LMDB|relay-set|relay set" docs/plan.md docs/aim.md docs/product-spec/subsystems.md docs/design/kernel-substrate.md docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md docs/design/reactivity/*.md' in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3464:docs/perf/codex-reviews/85770db.md:4807:docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3094:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2950:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1507:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3475:docs/perf/codex-reviews/85770db.md:4888:docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3510:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3366:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2070:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3500:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:90:+| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3502:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:255:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3503:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:614:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
