docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3547:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1326:+ | 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3548:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1550:++**Reading `signers/amber-clipboard-signer.ts:65-68`** (no specific commit, design observation). `visibilitychange` fires as soon as `window.open` is called. A naive `pendingRequest = createDefer()` immediately before the intent open would resolve with stale clipboard content. The workaround is a 500ms setTimeout before installing the pending request.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3551:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2053:++121 lines. Holds a `packageName` (the installed signer app id), a permissions list, cached pubkey. All methods delegate to `NostrSignerPlugin.<method>(packageName, payload, nanoid(), ...)` (lines 99-119). `setup()` (lines 49-61) calls `setPackageName` and `getPublicKey` once.

The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3609:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3657:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:29:| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |

Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:3692:docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2717:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2571:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2001:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8243:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:578:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2:2026-05-17T23:33:59.690224Z ERROR codex_core::session: failed to load skill /Users/pablofernandez/.agents/skills/voice-capture-sheet/SKILL.md: invalid YAML: mapping values are not allowed in this context at line 2 column 116
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:12:session id: 019e384a-150c-77e3-b8c3-5dbe73439deb
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:47: These remain to be resolved by ADRs after design review, not in this design pass.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:49:-1. **Plan-id stability under perturbation.** The intro/compiler picks "logical-interest set + author-mailbox snapshot ⇒ plan-id." That ties plan-id to mailbox membership, so a single new kind:10002 arrival reshuffles plan-ids for every interest including that author. An alternative scopes plan-id to the *logical-interest set only* and tracks per-relay assignment as a separate stable identifier. Pick one in an ADR; the test contracts in §9 assume the former.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:55: 2. **Filter-merge lattice formal-isation.** §3 step 3 lists which filter fields are safely mergeable across logical interests (`authors`, `kinds`, `since`, `until`, `limit`, tag operators). It does not yet specify the merge algebra for the corner case where two interests differ only in `limit` but agree on authors and kinds. Worth an ADR-level note before the lattice is coded; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2 promises "a formal merge lattice for `limit`, `since`, `until`, multi-filter arrays, and tag operators."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:56: 3. **Per-author indexer-fallback ledger row?** Today the compiler treats indexer fallback as an inline relay assignment. If the kind:10002 fetch is its own durable action (M6 ledger), the fallback becomes a tracked action with retry/cancel semantics. Cleaner for diagnostics; heavier for M2. Defer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:57: 4. **Read-relay vs write-relay use for subscriptions on the same author.** NIP-65 defines write relays (outbox) for the author's own events and read relays (inbox) for events directed *at* the author. For a `Timeline { authors: [...] }` we want write relays. For a `Notifications { p: [author] }` we want inbox relays. The compiler distinguishes them by filter shape (`authors` vs `#p`), matching the `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 routing table. Document a corner case: kind-1 filtered by both `authors` and `#p` is rare but real (replies to the author from the author). Pick a precedence in an ADR; current bias is `authors` wins (write relays).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:93:+a REQ; relay-count optimization is open question 8 (future ADR).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:127:-- **A new kind:10002 for a covered author changes plan-id.** Even if the author's new write relays overlap entirely with the old set (e.g. relay added then removed), the snapshot's `created_at` advanced, so the hash changes. ADR-0007 diagnostics will reflect this; the wire-emitter's diff will be a no-op if the actual relay assignment is unchanged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:142:-| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | Becomes `register_startup_interests()`: pushes 4 `LogicalInterest`s into the registry (seed timeline; account kind:0; account kind:10002; seed contacts kind:3). The compiler runs once and produces wire REQs. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) becomes an `InterestScope::Global` interest with hardcoded indexer routing flagged `RoutingSource::UserConfigured` (it is operator-debug seed data). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:146: | `open_firehose_tag` (requests.rs:170-200) | Registers one `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [1], tags: { #t: [tag] } }, scope: ActiveAccount, lifecycle: Tailing }`. Routes to active-account read relays per §3.1 table. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:165:+- It does not move social bootstrap (follow-list timeline, account kind:0/3/10002 fetches)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:176: The compiler's routing decisions are the most subtle correctness surface in the M2 milestone. They are also the easiest to silently get wrong (`docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §3, "automatic behaviour also needs strong tests"). Diagnostics make the four sources of relay knowledge legible — separately, never collapsed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:254:+3. `UserConfiguredRelayFact` records — increments/decrements `by_lane.user_configured` per (relay_url) membership. Records with `category == Indexer` contribute to `by_lane.indexer_fallback` (a sub-count of `user_configured`); the UI can show both the aggregate and the Indexer-specific sub-total.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:365:-- Future `nmp-nip01::UpdateRelayList` (M6).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:367:-- The per-app generated enum — `MailboxesView` becomes one variant of `ViewSpec` in `nmp-app-<name>` per ADR-0010.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:369:+  `nmp-app-<name>` per ADR-0010.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:370:+- Future `nmp-nip01::UpdateRelayList` (M6) — imports `parse::parse_relay_list` for validation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:384:@@ -7,6 +7,13 @@ This section defines the **publish-side seam** the M2 milestone lands so the M6
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:386: This is the framing the parent index calls out as "design seam now, first concrete consumer in M6." Without this seam, M6 risks reinventing outbox routing inline.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:433:     pub active_account:   Option<AccountId>,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:496:-- **Step 2's "no indexer fallback for writes"** is the structural enforcement of the doctrine `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." A failed Step 2 surfaces in the action ledger as `Failed { reason: NoAuthorRelays }`, which the UI renders as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:503:+  NoAuthorRelays }` in the action ledger, rendered as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:527:-Outbox routing implication: if `pubkey` was previously routed to the indexer fallback set (Stage 2 read-fallback), the compiler now reassigns to the author's declared write relays. The wire-emitter closes the indexer REQ for that author's slice and opens a new REQ on the declared relays. ADR-0007 diagnostics reflect the route source flipping from `Indexer` to `Nip65`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:528:+Outbox routing implication: if `pubkey` was previously routed to the indexer fallback set (Stage 2 read-fallback), the compiler now reassigns to the author's declared write relays. The wire-emitter closes the indexer REQ for that author's slice and opens a new REQ on the declared relays. ADR-0007 diagnostics reflect the route source flipping from `UserConfigured::Indexer` to `Nip65`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:536: M6+ trigger. Some interests (private DMs in M9, NIP-42 challenge response in M5) only become routable once a signer is loaded for their account. M2 records the trigger shape; behaviour is no-op pre-M6.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:538:+Implementation note: **signer must be bound before emitting `SignerAvailable`** — NDK gotcha
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:540:+binding the signer causes reactive consumers to run with no-signer or stale-signer. NMP's
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:541:+actor must bind the signer to the `SessionState` synchronously before emitting this trigger.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:556:-> yet; ADR-0010's codegen must emit it before this assertion can compile. M2's
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:562:+> (ADR-0010). `AppActionMeta` does not exist yet — it is a M2 implementation-PR
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:563:+> deliverable. ADR-0010's codegen must emit it before this assertion can compile.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:567:+> dependency lands; the ignore comment must name the tracking ADR and impl-PR.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:678:    seam for the M6 write path.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:693:                                              SignerAvailable)

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:743:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:8:| 2026-05-18 01:30 | 0a | Advisor pass: broadcast safe-rebase-push protocol to all 6 running agents (avoid push race on master). T1 description updated to mandate worktree isolation + rebase-push protocol. Heartbeat cron rewritten (job 811003f1) with stronger triage rules (design→review→impl chain; M11 gated on M10.5 *empirical* pass, not just designed; debt triage uses both must-fix and ADR-defer lanes; DerivedData sprawl mitigation; orphan detection). Heartbeat runtime is session-only (durable flag ignored). 6 stale stashes from prior codex sessions dropped. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:744:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:745:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:10:| 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:746:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:11:| 2026-05-18 02:25 | 3 | Heartbeat #3. User landed major scope shift: M9 DMs + M12 Wallet → post-v1; M11.5 Highlighter rebuild + nmp-nip29 crate added; framework-magic contract (kind:3 auto-tracking, bunker://, new-nsec, NDK/Applesauce-style "just works") folded across M2/M6/M8. Committed as 9eace68 (`plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md`). Four parallel Opus explorers dispatched: T15 applesauce-explorer (`/private/tmp/nostr-research/applesauce`), T16 ndk-explorer (`/Users/pablofernandez/Work/NDK-nhlteu` + prior outbox-research/), T17 framework-magic-designer, T18 highlighter-explorer (`/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` + NIP-29). T8 codex-fixer-1 + T11 codex-fixer-2 both finished (commits fb139ab, f68a479, b4b6afa, 151c80c, d871767, 80217cc — five sequential codex-driven fix cycles). T8 worktree cleaned (acdef1454b384c4b2). DISK PRESSURE: master Volumes/Data hit 96%; purged 6 stale `Podcastr-*` DerivedData dirs from prior podcast-rmp sessions (recovered ~7GB); m1-hardener notified to use `-derivedDataPath` going forward. T12 (clippy clean) dispatched (clippy-cleaner). Holding T13/T14 (codex fixes for M2/M3 designs) until current wave settles to avoid rebase storm. 7 agents in flight: m1-hardener, plan-splitter, applesauce-explorer, ndk-explorer, framework-magic-designer, highlighter-explorer, clippy-cleaner. User asleep — autonomous mode armed (cron f7c9fecf). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:747:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:748:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:13:| 2026-05-18 03:25 | 5 | Heartbeat #5 (autonomous). T10 plan-splitter landed ab632c1 + b49f8f3 (plan.md split into 25 per-milestone files + scope folded; codex follow-up clean). T11 codex-fixer-2 final commit (446a212) landed. T12 clippy-cleaner finished (1e358e1.md review clean, 8 lints fixed). T17 framework-magic-designer transcribed already landed. Codex review of cumulative HB4 diff flagged 6 substantive issues, biggest being a DOCTRINE VOCABULARY COLLISION I had introduced — D2/D3/D5 wording was inconsistent across README + framework-magic vs canonical product-spec/overview-and-dx.md §1.5 (which has D2 negentropy / D3 outbox / D5 snapshots-bounded). Logged as PD-001 in pending-user-decisions.md; README aligned in 3f5a2b3; T19 dispatched to align framework-magic docs. Also dispatched T20 (catch up missing codex review commits to make README claim "codex on every merge" true). Also dispatched T21 (research NDK svelte $metaSubscribe per user directive — Opus). T13 (M2 fix) dispatched this tick. Shell drift bug: pwd had silently switched into m1-hardener's worktree dir at start of heartbeat — recovered with explicit cd. Worktree cleanups: 5 completed workers (a114ff4e9/a983d7768/a9713e6e/a9c828b0/afbb948b) removed + T10 (a1da29d) removed. DerivedData 5.9G, disk 95% — within tolerance, no purge needed. 6 agents in flight: m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, framework-magic-reconciler, codex-review-catchup, meta-subscribe-analyst, m2-codex-fixer. Pending T14 (M3 fix) held until queue clears. |

Four parallel Opus explorers dispatched: T15 applesauce-explorer (`/private/tmp/nostr-research/applesauce`), T16 ndk-explorer (`/Users/pablofernandez/Work/NDK-nhlteu` + prior outbox-research/), T17 framework-magic-designer, T18 highlighter-explorer (`/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` + NIP-29). T8 codex-fixer-1 + T11 codex-fixer-2 both finished (commits fb139ab, f68a479, b4b6afa, 151c80c, d871767, 80217cc — five sequential codex-driven fix cycles). T8 worktree cleaned (acdef1454b384c4b2). DISK PRESSURE: master Volumes/Data hit 96%; purged 6 stale `Podcastr-*` DerivedData dirs from prior podcast-rmp sessions (recovered ~7GB); m1-hardener notified to use `-derivedDataPath` going forward. T12 (clippy clean) dispatched (clippy-cleaner). Holding T13/T14 (codex fixes for M2/M3 designs) until current wave settles to avoid rebase storm. 7 agents in flight: m1-hardener, plan-splitter, applesauce-explorer, ndk-explorer, framework-magic-designer, highlighter-explorer, clippy-cleaner. User asleep — autonomous mode armed (cron f7c9fecf). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:754:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

Codex review of cumulative HB4 diff flagged 6 substantive issues, biggest being a DOCTRINE VOCABULARY COLLISION I had introduced — D2/D3/D5 wording was inconsistent across README + framework-magic vs canonical product-spec/overview-and-dx.md §1.5 (which has D2 negentropy / D3 outbox / D5 snapshots-bounded). Logged as PD-001 in pending-user-decisions.md; README aligned in 3f5a2b3; T19 dispatched to align framework-magic docs. Also dispatched T20 (catch up missing codex review commits to make README claim "codex on every merge" true). Also dispatched T21 (research NDK svelte $metaSubscribe per user directive — Opus). T13 (M2 fix) dispatched this tick. Shell drift bug: pwd had silently switched into m1-hardener's worktree dir at start of heartbeat — recovered with explicit cd. Worktree cleanups: 5 completed workers (a114ff4e9/a983d7768/a9713e6e/a9c828b0/afbb948b) removed + T10 (a1da29d) removed. Pending T14 (M3 fix) held until queue clears. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1068:docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:15:2. **Type-erased registry.** Modules register handlers keyed by string namespace; payloads cross FFI as `Vec<u8>` (or `serde_json::Value`). The kernel doesn't know module types statically; it dispatches via the registry. Plug-and-play module loading; loses type safety at the FFI boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1069:docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:108:    Module(&'static str, Vec<u8>),    // module namespace + serialized action
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1070:docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:146:- **Cleaner FFI surface.** No `Vec<u8>` blobs across FFI. No JSON parsing on the platform side. No string-keyed switches.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1071:docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:157:The type-erased path (`Vec<u8>` actions, string-keyed registry, runtime dispatch) was seriously considered and rejected for v1:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1072:docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:159:- **Loses safety where it matters most.** The FFI boundary is exactly where strong typing has the highest payoff. Swift / Kotlin / TypeScript consumers benefit from idiomatic typed enums. Vec<u8> + opaque dispatch reproduces JavaScript-shaped fragility in three statically-typed languages.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1073:docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:177:| Type-erased registry (`Vec<u8>` actions) | See "Why not type-erased" above. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1270:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:261:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1154:412:+| Blocking debt markers (TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK/panic!/unimplemented!/todo!) | 0 | ✓ Clean |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1271:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:262:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1162:40:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1272:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:263:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1163:58:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1273:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:264:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1241:- [docs/plan.md:363](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/plan.md:363): “or has an ADR justifying the deferral” weakens the hard “zero open TODO/FIXME/XXX/unimplemented” gate. Fix: remove the deferral escape for scoped files.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1274:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:265:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1248:No executable TODO/FIXME/unimplemented debt was added in the changed files; the hits are doc text. No tests are needed for the plan/log docs themselves, but the audit doc must not be treated as empirical M10.5 coverage.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1308:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:299:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1940:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1331:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:322:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1230:+`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1339:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:330:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2124:docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:66:`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1343:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:334:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2196:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1345:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:336:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:178:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1436:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:427:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3477:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:846:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1437:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:428:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3478:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:848:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1438:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:429:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3480:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:850:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1469:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:460:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/151c80c.md:559:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:281:- **`// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO` blocks are the only allowed change** to a view file before it is wired. Any other source change requires an explicit ADR + a parity-screenshots.md note with reviewer sign-off.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1473:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:464:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:240:-- **1a.1** **Kernel substrate prototype + non-Nostr fixture** — the five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`); composite reverse index; delta buffer with coalescing; claim-based GC; codegen producing a working `nmp-app-fixture` crate from a `fixture-todo-core` app module that implements all five trait families; desktop iced shell rendering a TODO list. ~2 weeks. **Proves: kernel substrate works for non-Nostr-shaped data; codegen pipeline functional.**

Replace with a concrete researched protocol decision or scope RSS-only/Nostr-feed fallback explicitly.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1509:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:500:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1984:+docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1514:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:505:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5869:+docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1578:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:569:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6160:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3461:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:601:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1584:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:575:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6166:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3477:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:846:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1585:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:576:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6167:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3478:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:848:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1586:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:577:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6168:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3480:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:850:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1652:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:643:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6557:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:852:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1675:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:666:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8173:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1797:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:788:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1686:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3461:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:601:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1798:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:789:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1687:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3462:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:619:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1803:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:794:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1692:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3477:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:846:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1804:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:795:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1693:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3478:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:848:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1805:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:796:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1694:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3480:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:850:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1879:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:870:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2097:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1152:412:+| Blocking debt markers (TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK/panic!/unimplemented!/todo!) | 0 | ✓ Clean |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1880:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:871:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2098:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1160:40:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1881:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:872:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2099:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1161:58:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1882:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:873:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2100:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1239:- [docs/plan.md:363](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/plan.md:363): “or has an ADR justifying the deferral” weakens the hard “zero open TODO/FIXME/XXX/unimplemented” gate. Fix: remove the deferral escape for scoped files.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1883:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:874:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2101:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1246:No executable TODO/FIXME/unimplemented debt was added in the changed files; the hits are doc text. No tests are needed for the plan/log docs themselves, but the audit doc must not be treated as empirical M10.5 coverage.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1884:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:875:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2102:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1256:- [docs/plan.md:363](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/plan.md:363): “or has an ADR justifying the deferral” weakens the hard “zero open TODO/FIXME/XXX/unimplemented” gate. Fix: remove the deferral escape for scoped files.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1907:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:898:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/59f8d1b.md:159:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:281:- **`// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO` blocks are the only allowed change** to a view file before it is wired. Any other source change requires an explicit ADR + a parity-screenshots.md note with reviewer sign-off.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1914:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:905:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1552:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1933:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:924:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:2108:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6166:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3477:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:846:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1951:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:942:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:2710:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:240:-- **1a.1** **Kernel substrate prototype + non-Nostr fixture** — the five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`); composite reverse index; delta buffer with coalescing; claim-based GC; codegen producing a working `nmp-app-fixture` crate from a `fixture-todo-core` app module that implements all five trait families; desktop iced shell rendering a TODO list. ~2 weeks. **Proves: kernel substrate works for non-Nostr-shaped data; codegen pipeline functional.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:1960:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:951:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:2822:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:848:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2004:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:995:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:5334:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1692:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3477:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:846:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.

A failed Step 2 surfaces in the action ledger as `Failed { reason: NoAuthorRelays }`, which the UI renders as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2162:docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:189:Non-Nostr data must not be encoded as fake Nostr events. It enters one of four actor-owned lanes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2163:docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:198:The actor decides which lane a capability result belongs to. Native reports raw facts only; Rust derives policy.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2164:docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:242:- A synthetic non-Nostr module test writes domain records, action-ledger rows, capability reports, and side effects, then verifies each crosses the correct bridge lane.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2165:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2:2026-05-17T22:42:25.262680Z ERROR codex_core::session: failed to load skill /Users/pablofernandez/.agents/skills/voice-capture-sheet/SKILL.md: invalid YAML: mapping values are not allowed in this context at line 2 column 116
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2174:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:78:§7; extends ADR-0007 diagnostic records without collapsing the four lanes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2175:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:95:+> **Companion docs:** `docs/plan.md` §M2; `docs/aim.md` §4.4–§4.5; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7; ADR-0007 (diagnostic lanes); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) and §4 (`ActionModule`); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2–§7.3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2180:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:127:+1. **Plan-id stability under perturbation.** The intro/compiler picks "logical-interest set + author-mailbox snapshot ⇒ plan-id." That ties plan-id to mailbox membership, so a single new kind:10002 arrival reshuffles plan-ids for every interest including that author. An alternative scopes plan-id to the *logical-interest set only* and tracks per-relay assignment as a separate stable identifier. Pick one in an ADR; the test contracts in §9 assume the former.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2184:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:294:+- **A new kind:10002 for a covered author changes plan-id.** Even if the author's new write relays overlap entirely with the old set (e.g. relay added then removed), the snapshot's `created_at` advanced, so the hash changes. ADR-0007 diagnostics will reflect this; the wire-emitter's diff will be a no-op if the actual relay assignment is unchanged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2187:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:305:+| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | Becomes `register_startup_interests()`: pushes 4 `LogicalInterest`s into the registry (seed timeline; account kind:0; account kind:10002; seed contacts kind:3). The compiler runs once and produces wire REQs. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) becomes an `InterestScope::Global` interest with hardcoded indexer routing flagged `RoutingSource::UserConfigured` (it is operator-debug seed data). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2194:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:373:+Each lane has one record type. All four are exposed to the platform via the existing ADR-0007 `ViewBatch` lane (low-cadence, coalesced to 1–4 Hz per ADR-0007 "How status crosses the bridge"). They feed into the diagnostics screen, not into normal product UI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2199:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:428:+Emitted by `handle_event` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:134-164`) for every EVENT arrival. This is the highest-cardinality lane and the only one where coalescing matters at the ADR-0007 boundary: the platform diagnostic view consumes a summarised projection (`ProvenanceSummary` per author or per event), not the raw fact stream.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2200:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:452:+The compiler may *use* facts from multiple lanes to compute a routing decision; it may **not** present them as one. Concretely:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2201:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:454:+- **Routing decision** (`AuthorRouting.source` from [compiler.md](compiler.md) §3.1): records *which lane* the relay set was derived from. Single-valued; one of `Nip65 | Hint | Indexer | UserConfigured`. The author may have facts in three lanes; the compiler picks one, says so, and the other lane records remain visible.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2205:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:466:+- `User-configured` is the **only** lane the compiler treats as authoritative-by-policy (the user said so). Open question 5 in the parent index resolves the augment-vs-override precedence between NIP-65 and user-configured for the active account.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2206:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:468:+The lesson the four-lane discipline preserves: routing is **derivable but contested** evidence. Collapsing the lanes loses information; preserving them lets the diagnostic answer "why did we route this REQ to that relay?" months after the decision.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2210:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:522:+Emission cadence follows ADR-0007's diagnostic-view rule: material-transition immediately, otherwise 1–4 Hz. The provenance counter ticks every second; the `by_lane` counts only emit on `CompiledPlan` recompiles or new mailbox arrivals.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2218:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:573:+    /// Account-scoped interests resolve via the active account's mailbox view
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2227:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:592:+    /// Bound to the active account in SessionState. Re-routes on account switch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2228:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:594:+    /// Bound to a specific account (multi-account UIs, M8). Re-routes on that
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2229:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:595:+    /// account's mailbox refresh; ignored on account switch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2230:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:597:+    /// No account context. Used for global pointer loaders, NIP-19-driven
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2234:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:631:+    /// recompilation triggers that the view module opts into (account-switch,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2236:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:661:+- A **diagnostic record**. ADR-0007 lanes are facts derived from the planner's state, not inputs.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2256:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1029:+- **Step 2's "no indexer fallback for writes"** is the structural enforcement of the doctrine `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." A failed Step 2 surfaces in the action ledger as `Failed { reason: NoAuthorRelays }`, which the UI renders as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2259:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1103:+This is the SideEffect-lane payload per ADR-0007. The platform diagnostic UI renders an entry on every override; the count over a session is a soft metric (Cargo dependents using overrides routinely should re-examine their flow).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2287:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1621:/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "TODO|FIXME|unimplemented|panic!|todo!|unwrap'"\\(|TBD|stub|placeholder|does not exist yet|proposed|deferred|open question|future|post-M2|M6|M3|M4|M5|M8|M9\" docs/design/subscription-compilation.md docs/design/subscription-compilation" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2290:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1671:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:62:    /// Bound to a specific account (multi-account UIs, M8). Re-routes on that
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2291:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1698:/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "D0|D1|D2|D3|D4|D5|Doctrine|kernel never grows|single writer|capabilities report|M2|subscription compiler|subscription compilation|outbox|NIP-65|watermark|LMDB|relay-set|relay set" docs/plan.md docs/aim.md docs/product-spec/subsystems.md docs/design/kernel-substrate.md docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md docs/design/reactivity/*.md' in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2294:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1755:docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2298:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1940:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2301:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2037:docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:48:`AppConfig` is a `uniffi::Record` containing only platform-resolved primitives (paths, lists of relay URLs, feature-flag booleans). No `Arc<dyn ...>` types in the config — capabilities are registered separately via setters so each can be bridged on its own schedule.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2351:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2899:   128	- `User-configured` is the **only** lane the compiler treats as authoritative-by-policy (the user said so). Open question 5 in the parent index resolves the augment-vs-override precedence between NIP-65 and user-configured for the active account.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2352:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2901:   130	The lesson the four-lane discipline preserves: routing is **derivable but contested** evidence. Collapsing the lanes loses information; preserving them lets the diagnostic answer "why did we route this REQ to that relay?" months after the decision.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2356:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2955:   184	Emission cadence follows ADR-0007's diagnostic-view rule: material-transition immediately, otherwise 1–4 Hz. The provenance counter ticks every second; the `by_lane` counts only emit on `CompiledPlan` recompiles or new mailbox arrivals.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2375:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3410:     5	> **Companion docs:** `docs/plan.md` §M2; `docs/aim.md` §4.4–§4.5; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7; ADR-0007 (diagnostic lanes); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) and §4 (`ActionModule`); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2–§7.3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2380:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3442:    37	1. **Plan-id stability under perturbation.** The intro/compiler picks "logical-interest set + author-mailbox snapshot ⇒ plan-id." That ties plan-id to mailbox membership, so a single new kind:10002 arrival reshuffles plan-ids for every interest including that author. An alternative scopes plan-id to the *logical-interest set only* and tracks per-relay assignment as a separate stable identifier. Pick one in an ADR; the test contracts in §9 assume the former.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2388:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3491:    41	    /// Account-scoped interests resolve via the active account's mailbox view

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2399:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3513:    63	    /// account's mailbox refresh; ignored on account switch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2400:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3515:    65	    /// No account context. Used for global pointer loaders, NIP-19-driven
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2404:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3549:    99	    /// recompilation triggers that the view module opts into (account-switch,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2406:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3579:   129	- A **diagnostic record**. ADR-0007 lanes are facts derived from the planner's state, not inputs.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2411:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3894:docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:166:| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | Becomes `register_startup_interests()`: pushes 4 `LogicalInterest`s into the registry (seed timeline; account kind:0; account kind:10002; seed contacts kind:3). The compiler runs once and produces wire REQs. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) becomes an `InterestScope::Global` interest with hardcoded indexer routing flagged `RoutingSource::UserConfigured` (it is operator-debug seed data). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2412:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3904:docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:132:- **Step 2's "no indexer fallback for writes"** is the structural enforcement of the doctrine `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." A failed Step 2 surfaces in the action ledger as `Failed { reason: NoAuthorRelays }`, which the UI renders as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2553:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2333:docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:90:Emitted by `handle_event` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:134-164`) for every EVENT arrival. This is the highest-cardinality lane and the only one where coalescing matters at the ADR-0007 boundary: the platform diagnostic view consumes a summarised projection (`ProvenanceSummary` per author or per event), not the raw fact stream.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2562:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2460:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:56:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2716:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2570:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2000:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8242:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:537:+**Code Quality:** Zero bugs. All 20 code inspection findings are acceptable or justified by design. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2717:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2571:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2001:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8243:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:578:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2789:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2643:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2073:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8798:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:264:- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2809:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2663:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2093:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2847:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2701:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2131:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:395:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2849:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2703:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2133:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:422:+1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2854:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2708:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2138:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:754:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2857:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2711:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2141:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1257: | 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:2858:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2712:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2142:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1258: | 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3006:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2860:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2557:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:264:- 📝 **Sign-off:** doctrine-review.md § D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3013:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2869:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:68:+**`nostr-lmdb` gives us** (per `docs.rs/nostr-lmdb`): `save_event(&Event)`, `event_by_id(&EventId)`, `check_id(&EventId) -> DatabaseEventStatus`, `query(Filter) -> Events`, `count(Filter)`, `delete(Filter)`, `wipe()`, `negentropy_items(Filter) -> Vec<(EventId, Timestamp)>`. It owns the page allocator, the LMDB environment, primary by-id store, indexes derived from `Filter`, replaceable / parameterized-replaceable supersession, and NIP-09 delete handling. It is the only mature Rust LMDB store for Nostr events with proven NIP-77 integration; reinventing it is a year of work that we will not recoup.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3014:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2870:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:74:+| Per-relay provenance (which relays delivered each event; first seen / last seen) | Out of scope; the crate models events, not their wire history | `subsystems.md` §7.1 "Duplicate id → merge relay provenance set"; ADR-0007 diagnostics; outbox routing scoring in M2+ |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3017:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2873:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:213:+On duplicate-id insert (§7.1 row 2), `insert()` does not touch the primary; it only updates the provenance sidecar (`last_seen_ms` bump on the matching `ProvenanceEntry`, or append). The "primary relay" — for outbox-routing scoring (M2) and ADR-0007 diagnostics — is deterministically the first relay observed (`sources[0]` after sort by `first_seen_ms`).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3042:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2898:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1428:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3070:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2926:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1466:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1755:docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3072:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2928:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1470:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1774:docs/plan.md:271:**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3077:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2933:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1476:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1940:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3082:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2938:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1489:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2112:docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:112:- **Subsequent expansion has a working substrate to build on.** Adding LMDB is a `Box<dyn EventStore>` swap. Adding multi-relay is a planner change. Adding negentropy is a planner change. Adding iOS is a UniFFI wrap of the existing actor. None require redesigning the architecture.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3094:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2950:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1507:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3095:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2951:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1508:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3251:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3107:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1686:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3461:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:601:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3258:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3114:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1693:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3478:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:848:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3259:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3115:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1694:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3480:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:850:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3269:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3125:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1704:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3515:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1152:412:+| Blocking debt markers (TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK/panic!/unimplemented!/todo!) | 0 | ✓ Clean |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3270:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3126:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1705:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3521:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1160:40:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3271:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3127:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1706:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3522:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1161:58:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:3272:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3128:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1707:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3527:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1239:- [docs/plan.md:363](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/plan.md:363): “or has an ADR justifying the deferral” weakens the hard “zero open TODO/FIXME/XXX/unimplemented” gate. Fix: remove the deferral escape for scoped files.

Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
