docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:1:# NMP M6 Sessions + Signers — Synthesis & Recommendation
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:11:a. **Encryption namespace** — mirror applesauce's optional capability pattern (`interop.ts:8-15`):
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:32:c. **Sync vs async:** all methods async (return `BoxFuture`). NDK's sync `pubkey` getter that can `throw "Not ready"` violates D6 — never replicate.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:36:### 1.2 Account/Session type — fields, mutable vs immutable
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:63:`AccountId` (ULID) distinct from `pubkey` — required because applesauce allows multiple accounts for one pubkey (`manager.ts:60-63`), and NMP must support "same nsec, different relay policy" or "same bunker user from two devices."
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:68:- Follow set, mute list, relay list **are not on the account** — query the domain stores by `pubkey`.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:120:LMDB stores the *index* — `AccountId → keyring_key` — plus the public account record (display name, namespace, pubkey, last_active, created_at). The keyring stores the secret material.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:128:**Applesauce's mechanism**: not implemented in the accounts package — the consumer subscribes via the EventStore and gets reactive results via rxjs query results.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:143:    pub relays: Vec<String>,       // ≥1
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:154:**Reconnect strategy:** on app restart, re-deserialize from keyring (which includes `local_signer.private_key` + `relay_urls` + `bunker_pubkey` + `user_pubkey`), re-open the subscription, but **do NOT re-issue `connect`** — the bunker already trusts this local signer (`index.ts:592-607`). This is the key insight: bunker pairing is per-local-signer-key, not per-session.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:158:**Secret parameter handling:** the secret is **only** valid for the initial `connect` call; it is NOT a long-term token. After ack, the bunker authorizes by `local_signer.pubkey`. The secret can be persisted in `BunkerUri.secret` (some bunkers re-validate on every reconnect; AlbyHub does this) — NDK persists it (`nip46/index.ts:547`), applesauce does too (`nostr-connect-account.ts:24-29`). **NMP: persist it.**
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:169:- The Rust side treats this exactly like `IdentityScopeKind::ExternalSigner` — no special case.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:184:| Paste `bunker://` URL → live signer | Hardware signers (Coldcard, Krux, etc.) — analogous to applesauce's SerialPortSigner |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:187:| Action ledger w/ atomic publish + store | Account-rotation / signer migration UX |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:190:**Critical M6 invariant:** one active account at a time is fine. Multi-account *visibility* (showing other accounts in a switcher) is M8. **But** the data model must already support multiple accounts in the keyring + LMDB so M8 is purely a UI/UX milestone — don't bake "single account" into any schema or actor message.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:201:| Signer-mismatch post-conditions | no | yes (`account.ts:115-128`) | **yes, mandatory** — add to NMP wrapper |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:216:| D0 (no app nouns in kernel) | "HumanAccount" / "Bunker" naming risks app-coupling | These are `IdentityModule` namespaces, not core enum variants. The kernel only knows `IdentityScopeKind::{HumanAccount, AppLocal, ExternalSigner, Ephemeral}` (`identity.rs:27-32`) — that's the right abstraction level. |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:221:| D5 (snapshots bounded by what's open) | Per-account `walletEnabled` opt-in is the right shape | NDK already does this — copy verbatim (`sessions/src/manager.ts:322-394`). |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:224:| D8 (reactivity contract) | Account-bootstrap subscriptions emit derived-store deltas | Per-domain-store reverse-index participation is the existing pattern — account bootstrap is just another producer. ≤60Hz cap applies uniformly. |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:230:- `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/identity.rs` — extend `IdentityError`; add `Nip04Capability`/`Nip44Capability` optional traits; add signer-wrapper with post-conditions.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:231:- `crates/nmp-core/src/identity/{registry,bunker_uri,nip49}.rs` (new) — type-tagged dispatch, bunker URL parser, NIP-49 encrypt/decrypt helpers.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:232:- `crates/nmp-modules/nmp-identity-human/` (new) — `HumanAccount` module (nsec + ncryptsec).
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:233:- `crates/nmp-modules/nmp-identity-bunker/` (new) — NIP-46 client implementation; rides relay pool.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:234:- `crates/nmp-core/src/keyring/` — `KeyringCapability` integration with account-record serde + opaque blob.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:235:- `crates/nmp-core/src/app/state.rs` — add `session: SessionState { accounts: Vec<AccountRecord>, active_pubkey: Option<Hexpubkey> }`.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:236:- `crates/nmp-core/src/actions/account.rs` (new) — handlers for `AddAccount*`, `ActivateAccount`, `RemoveAccount`, `GenerateNewAccount`.
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:243:NMP's actor model gives this for free: one `AccountId` is the unit of everything. The "session" is just "this account is currently active" — a single `Option<AccountId>` in `AppState`. Derived state lives in domain stores keyed by pubkey, queryable for any account whether active or not.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:1:# Applesauce Accounts + Signers — Deep Dive
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:7:- `packages/signers/src/interop.ts` (82 LOC) — the `ISigner` trait + connection-method plumbing.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:8:- `packages/signers/src/signers/*` — 10 signer implementations (8 production + 2 deprecated/aliases). Per-signer LOC table in `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md:24-33`.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:9:- `packages/accounts/src/account.ts` (188 LOC) — `BaseAccount` with the load-bearing per-account queue and signer-mismatch guards.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:10:- `packages/accounts/src/manager.ts` (193 LOC) — `AccountManager` with rxjs `BehaviorSubject` for reactive active-account.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:11:- `packages/accounts/src/proxy-signer.ts` (37 LOC) — the rxjs proxy that makes `manager.signer` stable across active-account swaps.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:12:- `packages/accounts/src/accounts/*` — 9 account adapters (Extension, Password, Readonly, PrivateKey, NostrConnect, AmberClipboard, AndroidNative, SerialPort, Simple).
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:14:**No "session" concept** in applesauce. The library deliberately does NOT track per-account derived state (followSet, muteSet, etc.) — those live in the consumer's `EventStore` and are queried per-render. This is the cleanest separation in the JS ecosystem.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:30:- `nip04`/`nip44` are **optional namespaces**, not required methods — extension/readonly signers can lack them entirely without any "Not supported" stubs.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:33:**Applies to NMP:** the smaller surface is right. NMP's `IdentityModule::sign` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/identity.rs:18-22`) currently takes `&UnsignedEvent` and returns `Result<SignedEvent, SigningError>` — already closer to applesauce's "return the full event" than NDK's "return sig only". **Keep this.**
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:35:**Applies to NMP:** the optional-namespace pattern for encryption (returning `Option<NipXxEncryptor>`) is what NMP's `IdentityModule` should grow into — different account types have genuinely different capability surfaces.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:53:- **`IAccount extends ISigner`**: the account IS the signer the rest of the app uses. There's no separate "give me the signer for this account" step — the account is the surface, and it wraps signer calls with queue+mismatch guards.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:55:- **`SerializedAccount`** (`types.ts:12-23`) is `{id, type, pubkey, signer, metadata?}` — explicitly typed by the account type's `SignerData` and `Metadata` generics.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:57:**Applies to NMP:** the "account wraps signer" pattern is the right shape for NMP's `IdentityModule::HumanAccount` etc. `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/identity.rs:11` has `Descriptor: Clone + Serialize + DeserializeOwned` — that's the analog of `IAccountConstructor.fromJSON` (line 51-53).
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:59:## 4. `BaseAccount` — three load-bearing details (`packages/accounts/src/account.ts`)
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:80:Every privileged call (`getPublicKey`, `signEvent`, `nip04.*`, `nip44.*`) routes through `operation() → waitForQueue` (lines 86-89, 60-73, 67-73, 107-128). Reasons captured in `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md:114-119`:
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:107:**Applies to NMP:** **MUST copy.** A malicious or buggy signer (compromised extension, broken bunker, hardware glitch) could otherwise sign events as a *different* user without detection. NMP's `IdentityModule::sign` wrapper must enforce both checks before returning `Ok(SignedEvent)` — currently no such assertion exists in `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/identity.rs`.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:111:Per-account app-level state with rxjs reactivity. Not serialized by default — the app's `toJSON` override decides whether to persist metadata. **Applies to NMP:** NMP can support "account custom name / color / muted relays" the same way — per-account metadata is a domain-store concern, not a kernel concern. Keep it out of the `IdentityModule` trait surface.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:123:    if (!account) throw new Error("Cant find account with that ID");
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:130:`active$` and `accounts$` are rxjs `BehaviorSubject`s — views subscribe and re-render on `.next(...)`. The de-duplication check (`!== account.id`) avoids redundant re-renders.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:152:`AccountManager` exposes `signer: ISigner` (line 21-28) which is a `ProxySigner` over `active$`. **What this enables:** consumers do `new EventFactory({ signer: manager.signer })` *once*, and the factory continues to "just work" when the user switches accounts — the proxy always re-dispatches to whatever is in `active$`. **No re-binding required.**
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:154:**Applies to NMP:** this is exactly the problem NMP avoids by having the actor own the signer binding internally. Native code in NMP never holds a signer reference — it issues `AppAction::SendNote { … }` and the actor uses the currently-active account. **Confirms NMP's existing design.**
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:165:fromJSON(accounts: SerializedAccount<any, Metadata>[], quite = false) {
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:168:        if (!AccountType) { if (!quite) throw new Error(`Missing account type ${json.type}`); else continue; }
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:177:**Applies to NMP:** type-tagged dispatch on a static `type` string is the same pattern NDK uses (`core/src/signers/registry.ts:7-14`). NMP's existing `IdentityModule::NAMESPACE` const (`identity.rs:9`) is the analog — confirm that the orchestrator code path uses this for serde dispatch.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:190:Caused the bug in commit `96120548` (cited in `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md:132`) — "ActionRunner cache causing actions to have wrong `user` when accounts change." **Applies to NMP:** signer-rotation (e.g., user reconnects a bunker with a new local key) must invalidate any in-flight actions bound to the old account. The actor must abort and re-route.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:192:## 6. Signer adapters — what's interesting
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:211:Three validations: `isHexKey(remote)`, at-least-one relay, secret is optional. NDK's parser (`core/src/signers/nip46/index.ts:204-215`) has the same hostname/pathname dance but does NOT validate isHex — **applesauce's version is stricter**.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:224:One static method, parse + construct + connect. **Applies to NMP:** the NIP-46 module should expose exactly this shape — `IdentityModule::ExternalSigner::create(IdentityDescriptor::BunkerUri(uri))` parses and dials, returning a fully-connected signer (or `IdentityError::InvalidDescriptor(...)`).
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:236:### 6.3 `switchRelays` (lines 413-430) — protocol extension
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:251:Mirror of NDK's `switch_relays` (`core/src/signers/nip46/index.ts:375-411`). Both libraries support this NIP-46 extension; both tear down + rebuild the subscription. **Applies to NMP:** support it from day one — bunkers actively use it (nsec.app, AlbyHub).
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:255:The reverse flow — the client publishes a `nostrconnect://` URI containing its local pubkey + secret + relays + metadata, and waits for any bunker to connect to it. Implementation in `helpers/nostr-connect.ts:156-167`. **Applies to NMP M6:** the spec mentions only `bunker://` (`docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:13`); `nostrconnect://` is an addition worth tracking but can be M7+ since the bunker-paste flow covers 95% of M6's exit criterion.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:261:### 6.6 `AmberClipboardSigner` — clipboard IPC (`amber-clipboard-signer.ts`)
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:263:Already covered in `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md:94-103`. Key takeaways: a `visibilitychange` listener with a 500ms guard against the immediate-fire race; `rejectPending` for canceling in-flight requests; the entire signer is unusable on non-Android (`SUPPORTED` check, line 12-17). **Applies to NMP:** any iOS-side Secure-Enclave / external-app signer must have the same "visible again → read response → settle pending promise" shape, with explicit cancellation on logout/switch.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:265:### 6.7 `AndroidNativeSigner` — Capacitor plugin (121 LOC)
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:269:### 6.8 `ExtensionSigner` — pubkey caching (36 LOC)
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:271:Lines 17, 22-28: pubkey cached after first `getPublicKey()` call (commit `0867a502`). The cache is **per-instance**, not per-pubkey — a fresh `new ExtensionSigner()` re-prompts. **Applies to NMP:** the wasm/web bridge (post-M15) should similarly cache; pubkey is `O(1)` to compute server-side but `O(user-attention)` if the extension prompts for confirmation.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:273:## 7. Persistence — applesauce vs NDK
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:280:| Storage adapter abstraction | None — `toJSON()`/`fromJSON()`, caller writes | `SessionStorage` interface (`storage/types.ts:8-23`) |
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:281:| Auto-save | None — explicit `manager.toJSON()` calls | Debounced 500ms auto-save (`manager.ts:467-477`) |
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:287:1. **`IAccount extends ISigner`** is the cleanest contract. NMP's `IdentityModule::sign` already takes `(ctx, id, unsigned)` — the account is implicit in `id`. Keep it.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:290:4. **Type-tagged registry** — `IdentityModule::NAMESPACE` is already in place; ensure serde dispatch uses it.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:294:8. **NostrConnect's `repeat() + retry()` for reconnect** maps to NMP's relay pool — the NIP-46 client must be a *consumer* of the pool, not a peer of it.
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:295:9. **`switchRelays` NIP-46 extension** — implement from M6. 5s timeout is the right default.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:1:# NDK Sessions + Signers — Deep Dive
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:9:- `sessions/src/*` (1.7k LOC) — pure session/signer state machine with pluggable storage. Built on `zustand/vanilla` (`store.ts:11`).
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:10:- `core/src/signers/*` — the `NDKSigner` trait + registry + four built-in signers (PrivateKey, NIP-07, NIP-46 client+backend); `mobile/src/signers/nip55.ts` adds the Android Amber bridge by calling `registerSigner("nip55", …)`.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:11:- `react/src/session/*` — a *parallel* zustand store that pre-dates the new `sessions` package and is still the React integration layer (`react/src/session/store/types.ts:16`). Diverges in field names (`NDKUserSession` vs `NDKSession`) and embeds profile resolution.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:13:**Applies to NMP:** the kernel must do the work of NDK's `sessions` package internally — there will be no separate per-frontend session store. Native shadows derive from `AppState` per D4.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:19:`sessions/src/types.ts:7-51` — what NDK keeps **per active account** (in memory):
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:25:    muteSet?: Map<string, string>;     // from kind:10000 ("p"|"e" → id)
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:47:Comment on the serializer (`sessions/src/persistence-manager.ts:65-67`): *"Only restores identity and signer — data will be fetched from relays/cache."*
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:49:**Applies to NMP:** the persistence boundary is principled — persist *identity + signer descriptor + user preferences*; everything else is derived from the event store at next start. This maps cleanly onto NMP's actor-owned domain stores: the kernel persists `AccountId` + `IdentityDescriptor` blob; follow set, mute set, relay list are derived from the verified event store.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:51:## 3. `NDKSessionManager` — the public API
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:55:- `login(userOrSigner, { setActive })` (line 147) — accepts a signer OR a bare user (read-only account). Returns the pubkey.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:56:- `createAccount({ profile, relays, wallet, follows }, { publish, signer })` (line 193) — generates an nsec, optionally publishes kind:0/10002/3 + sets up NIP-60 wallet.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:57:- `switchTo(pubkey | null)` (line 280) — null deactivates.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:58:- `logout(pubkey?)` (line 273) — defaults to active.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:59:- `startSession(pubkey, opts: SessionStartOptions)` (line 287) / `stopSession` (line 294) — control what kinds get subscribed for this pubkey.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:60:- `addMonitor([NDKEventConstructor | NDKKind, ...])` (line 307) — incremental kind-monitor extension on the active session.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:61:- `enableWallet(pubkey?)` / `disableWallet` / `isWalletEnabled` (lines 322–394) — *opt-in* fetching for NIP-60 wallet (kind:17375 etc.). Crucially, this **restarts the subscription** so wallet kinds are not over-fetched for accounts that won't use them.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:62:- `restore()` (line 413) — load + start subscriptions for restored sessions; honors per-session `walletEnabled` preference.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:63:- `subscribe(cb)` (line 406) — pass-through to zustand subscribe.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:67:**Applies to NMP:** `createAccount` packages a "generate + publish kind:0/10002/3 + opt-in wallet" flow into one call — matches M6's "Create new nsec flow" deliverable in `docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:14`.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:69:**Diverges from NMP:** NDK's manager owns NDK-instance side effects directly (`ndk.signer = …`, `ndk.muteFilter = …`). NMP's signer is selected by `ActivateAccount { pubkey }` (`docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:108`) and bound through the actor; no public assignment of a global "current signer" exists.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:71:## 4. The store layer — what reactivity buys you
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:96:The signer is wired *before* `activePubkey` flips so any zustand subscriber waking from the activePubkey change sees a fully-configured NDK. **Applies to NMP:** the actor's `ActivateAccount` handler must publish a single `AppUpdate::FullState` after *all* derived state is rebuilt — partial-update visibility is a class of bug NDK hit (the comment is "//prevents race conditions").
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:100:`store.ts:286-316` — `ndk.muteFilter` becomes a closure over the *session's* mute data; `ndk.relayConnectionFilter` becomes a closure over `blockedRelays`. Both are cleared on switch-to-null. **Applies to NMP:** the kernel's per-account filter sets must be rebound atomically with `ActivateAccount`; nothing outside the actor should hold a "current mute filter" reference.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:150:- `pubkey` getter is **sync** but can throw `"Not ready"` for NIP-07/NIP-46 — caller must call `blockUntilReady()` first. Documented at `index.ts:21-23`.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:157:**Applies to NMP:** errors-as-sentinels (`throw new Error("Not ready")`) is exactly what D6 forbids. NMP must return `Result<SignedEvent, SigningError>` (already does — `identity.rs:18-22, 72-76`).
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:163:Accepts hex *or* nsec via one ctor (line 43-58 — note the `nsec1`/length-64 sniff). Adds NIP-49 helpers `encryptToNcryptsec(password, logn, ksb)` (line 111) and `fromNcryptsec(ncryptsec, password)` (line 140). `toPayload` (line 211) stores the hex key in plaintext — **explicit choice not to wrap in NIP-49 at the persistence layer**; that's the consumer's responsibility.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:171:- **Encryption queue** (`encryptionQueue` line 34, `processEncryptionQueue` line 169) — serializes encrypt/decrypt calls and retries on `"call already executing"` with backoff. Without this, two concurrent encrypts to a NIP-07 extension race.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:172:- `toPayload` (line 253) stores only `{"type":"nip07","payload":""}` — there's nothing to persist; on restore a fresh `NDKNip07Signer` is constructed and re-prompts.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:201:Note: `hostname || pathname.replace(/^\/\//, "")` to defend against Firefox vs Chrome differences in WHATWG URL parsing (also explicitly called out in applesauce — see `applesauce-sessions.md` §6). The `secret` parameter is forwarded to the bunker's `connect` RPC (`index.ts:347-351`) — it is the bunker's challenge-response handshake.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:205:`switchRelays` (`index.ts:375-411`): a NIP-46 protocol extension where the bunker can tell the client which relays to migrate to. Times out at 5s if the bunker doesn't reply — important so old/simple bunkers don't hang the login.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:207:`toPayload` (`index.ts:536-553`) persists `bunkerPubkey + userPubkey + relayUrls + secret + localSignerPayload`. `fromPayload` (`index.ts:562-610`) re-subscribes and is ready to sign — **does not re-issue `connect`** because the bunker already trusts this `localSigner`. This is the "reconnect after app restart" path.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:209:**NDKNip46TimeoutError** (`index.ts:21-33`) — typed timeout error with the operation name. `signer.timeout` is opt-in (default undefined). **Applies to NMP:** the `IdentityError` enum in `identity.rs:50-53` needs a `Timeout(operation, ms)` variant (currently only has `InvalidDescriptor` and `Storage`).
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:213:Android Amber bridge via `expo-nip55`. Important shape: stores `packageName` + cached `_pubkey` in `toPayload` (line 139) so re-creating the signer doesn't re-prompt the user for app selection. **Applies to NMP:** the Apple Secure Enclave / iOS-passkey signer (post-M6) will have the same shape — store an opaque platform-side handle (`packageName` analog) plus cached pubkey; on restore, the platform-side capability uses that handle to re-establish the link.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:227:`LocalStorage` (`local-storage.ts`, 59 LOC) — `localStorage[key] = JSON.stringify(...)`. Throws `StorageError("localStorage is not available")` outside browsers (line 14-17). `FileStorage` (`file-storage.ts`, 56 LOC) — dynamic-imports `fs/promises` so it tree-shakes out of browser bundles. `MemoryStorage` for tests. **No platform-native (Keychain/Keystore) storage** — those are explicitly the consumer's job, same boundary as NMP's `KeyringCapability`.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:229:`PersistenceManager` (`persistence-manager.ts`) is the orchestration layer. Save (line 43-51) serializes all sessions, writes. Restore (line 23-39) loads, recreates sessions WITHOUT activating, then activates the saved active pubkey. **Important:** `restoreSession` (line 68-95) tolerates a missing signer payload — the session re-appears as read-only if the signer fails to deserialize (e.g. NIP-07 extension uninstalled, NIP-46 bunker pubkey rotated). This is how a "lost signer" downgrades gracefully to npub-only mode rather than crashing the session.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:231:**Applies to NMP:** The kernel must do the same — a startup-time signer-resurrection failure must downgrade to read-only and surface a toast (per D6), not break the session entirely.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:235:Six typed errors all extending `SessionError extends Error`. The discriminator is `error.name` (set in each constructor). `SignerDeserializationError`, `StorageError`, `SessionNotFoundError`, `NoActiveSessionError`, `NDKNotInitializedError`. **Applies to NMP:** NMP's `IdentityError` enum (`identity.rs:50-53`) currently has only two variants; the equivalent set is needed when M6 lands — at minimum `NotFound(pubkey)`, `NotActive`, `SignerRestoreFailed(reason)`, `KeyringUnavailable`, `Timeout(op, ms)`.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:250:Subscribes to zustand store, debounces a save by `saveDebounceMs` (default 500ms, `manager.ts:65`). Save errors are *logged*, not propagated — partial-failure isolation. **Applies to NMP:** session persistence in NMP should be similarly debounced (the actor can use a heartbeat tick rather than a JS debounce). Errors become toast state per D6, not bubbled to the platform.
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:252:## 10. The React layer (`react/src/session/store/*`) — divergence note
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:256:- `NDKUserSession` (`react/src/session/store/types.ts:16-38`) carries `profile?: NDKUserProfile` directly on the session — the new `sessions/types.ts:7` does NOT (profile is its own concern).
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:257:- `switchToUser` in react (`react/src/session/store/switch-to-user.ts:13-70`) couples with a separate `useNDKMutes` store and a `useNDKStore.getState().setSigner(signer)` — i.e., the signer lives in yet another zustand atom. This is the "monolithic Nostr client object quietly accumulating concerns" pattern NMP's lessons doc warns against (`docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:31`).
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:261:## 11. NDK summary table — what to copy, what to skip
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:265:| Type-tagged signer registry | `core/src/signers/registry.ts:7-14` | **copy** — fits `IdentityModule::Descriptor` |
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:267:| Persist identity+signer only; derive everything else from events | `persistence-manager.ts:65-67` | **copy** — matches D4 |
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:268:| Per-session opt-in fetch toggles (`follows`, `mutes`, `wallet`, …) | `manager.ts:287, 322, 356` | **copy** — fits ViewSpec + D5 |
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:270:| Storage adapter interface w/ memory/file/local impls | `storage/types.ts:8-23` | **copy** — but NMP's adapter is LMDB + `KeyringCapability` |
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:271:| `NDKNip46TimeoutError` typed timeout | `nip46/index.ts:21-33` | **copy** — add to `IdentityError` |
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:272:| `restoreSession` tolerates missing signer → read-only | `persistence-manager.ts:75-88` | **copy** as actor behavior |
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:273:| zustand-vanilla as primitive | `store.ts:11, 98` | **skip** — NMP uses actor + `AppUpdate` |
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:274:| Global `ndk.signer = …` mutation | `store.ts:282` | **skip** — NMP uses `ActivateAccount` intent |
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:275:| Direct `ndk.muteFilter = (event) => …` closure | `store.ts:286-302` | **skip** — NMP filters live in domain stores |
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:276:| Profile on session struct | `react/src/session/store/types.ts:18` | **skip** — profile is its own domain store |
docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md:277:| React package's parallel store | `react/src/session/store/*` | **anti-pattern** — never repeat |

docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:275:| Aspect | applesauce | NDK |
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:276:| --- | --- | --- |
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:277:| What's persisted | `{id, type, pubkey, signer: SignerData, metadata}` per account | `{pubkey, signerPayload, lastActive, preferences}` per session |
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:278:| Active marker | NOT persisted by `AccountManager.toJSON()` (lines 143-155) | Persisted as `activePubkey` (`storage/local-storage.ts:19`) |
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:279:| Derived state (followSet, mutes) | NOT persisted, NOT tracked by account layer | NOT persisted, but tracked in-memory by session (`types.ts:9-30`) |
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:280:| Storage adapter abstraction | None — `toJSON()`/`fromJSON()`, caller writes | `SessionStorage` interface (`storage/types.ts:8-23`) |
docs/research/sessions/applesauce-sessions.md:281:| Auto-save | None — explicit `manager.toJSON()` calls | Debounced 500ms auto-save (`manager.ts:467-477`) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:112:| Lives in LMDB (`nmp-core` event store / domain stores) | Lives in `KeyringCapability` | In-memory only |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:113:| --- | --- | --- |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:114:| All received events (kind:0, 3, 10000, 10001, 10002, etc.) | Encrypted `AccountRecord` per account (one keyring entry per `AccountId`) | `ActiveAccount` (derived on activate) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:115:| `FollowSet`, `MuteSet`, `RelayList` per pubkey (derived domain stores) | Encrypted bunker `local_signer` private keys (with the bunker descriptor) | Subscription handles per active account |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:116:| Last-active timestamp per account (so resume picks "most recent") | NIP-49-encrypted nsec bytes | Action ledger entries (already specified in M6) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:180:| In scope for M6 | Deferred (M7+) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:181:| --- | --- |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:182:| Paste nsec (raw + ncryptsec) | `nostrconnect://` (client-initiated reverse handshake) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:183:| Generate new nsec + NIP-49 encrypt + keyring store | Multi-account switcher UI (M8 per `docs/plan/m8-multi-account.md`) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:184:| Paste `bunker://` URL → live signer | Hardware signers (Coldcard, Krux, etc.) — analogous to applesauce's SerialPortSigner |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:185:| `KeychainCapability` real impl on iOS | NIP-07 web extension (M15) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:186:| Active account binding (one at a time) | Account metadata (color, display name editing) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:187:| Action ledger w/ atomic publish + store | Account-rotation / signer migration UX |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:188:| Compose flow on iOS | NIP-55 / Amber-style iOS-app deeplink signers |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:194:| Dimension | NDK | applesauce | NMP recommendation |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:195:| --- | --- | --- | --- |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:196:| Signer trait size | 9 required + 1 optional method | 2 required + 2 optional namespaces | 3 required + 2 optional (matches applesauce) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:197:| Return shape for sign | sig string only | full signed event | full signed event (already in `identity.rs:65-69`) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:198:| Sync `pubkey` accessor | yes, throws if not ready | no, async only | async only |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:199:| Type-tagged signer registry | yes (`registry.ts:7-14`) | yes (`manager.ts:33-42`) | `IdentityModule::NAMESPACE` (already in `identity.rs:9`) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:200:| Per-account queue | no (NIP-07 has its own encrypt queue) | yes, mandatory (`account.ts:137-187`) | yes, per-account actor inbox serializes |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:201:| Signer-mismatch post-conditions | no | yes (`account.ts:115-128`) | **yes, mandatory** — add to NMP wrapper |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:202:| Bunker URL parser | regex hostname/pathname (no hex check) | URL + isHexKey validation | strict hex validation |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:203:| `switch_relays` NIP-46 ext | yes, 5s timeout (`nip46/index.ts:375-411`) | yes, async retry (`nostr-connect-signer.ts:413-430`) | yes, 5s timeout |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:204:| Reactive active-account | zustand store subscription | rxjs BehaviorSubject | `AppUpdate::FullState`/`SessionDelta` only |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:205:| Persist active marker | yes | no | yes (UX-friendly) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:206:| Auto-save | debounced 500ms | none (explicit `toJSON`) | debounced via actor tick |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:207:| Derived state location | on session struct | not in account package | own domain stores |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:208:| Storage adapter trait | yes (`SessionStorage`) | no | `KeyringCapability` + LMDB are NMP's "adapters" |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:209:| `replaceAccount` (signer rotation) | logout+login | explicit method | dedicated `RotateAccountSigner` action; aborts in-flight |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:210:| Read-only fallback on signer-restore-fail | yes (`persistence-manager.ts:75-88`) | implicit via ReadonlySigner | yes, with toast (D6) |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:214:| Doctrine | Risk vector | Mitigation |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:215:| --- | --- | --- |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:216:| D0 (no app nouns in kernel) | "HumanAccount" / "Bunker" naming risks app-coupling | These are `IdentityModule` namespaces, not core enum variants. The kernel only knows `IdentityScopeKind::{HumanAccount, AppLocal, ExternalSigner, Ephemeral}` (`identity.rs:27-32`) — that's the right abstraction level. |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:217:| D1 (best-effort rendering) | Account display-name takes a moment to load on switch | `ActiveAccount.display_name: Option<String>` is a placeholder field; view payload renders pubkey-truncation until loaded. |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:218:| D2 (negentropy first) | Account-bootstrap subscriptions are REQ-based | First fetch of kind:0/3/10002 is REQ; subsequent reconciliation can use NIP-77 (M7+). |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:219:| D3 (outbox auto) | Bootstrap subscription needs to know the user's outbox before having read kind:10002 | Bootstrap goes to default-relay list on first activation; rewires to outbox once kind:10002 lands. **Same idempotent re-compile pattern as views.** |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:220:| D4 (single writer per fact) | NDK's session struct holds derived state | NMP splits: domain stores own derived state; account record owns identity only. |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:221:| D5 (snapshots bounded by what's open) | Per-account `walletEnabled` opt-in is the right shape | NDK already does this — copy verbatim (`sessions/src/manager.ts:322-394`). |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:222:| D6 (errors never cross FFI) | `IdentityError` is too narrow today | Extend per §1.1. Every error becomes a toast field. |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:223:| D7 (capabilities report) | `KeychainCapability::store` is one of the most "decision-shaped" capabilities | The capability stores opaque bytes; NMP decides what to encrypt and what key derivation to use. Per `api-surface.md:198-203` this is already correct. |
docs/research/sessions/synthesis.md:224:| D8 (reactivity contract) | Account-bootstrap subscriptions emit derived-store deltas | Per-domain-store reverse-index participation is the existing pattern — account bootstrap is just another producer. ≤60Hz cap applies uniformly. |

     1	import type NDK from "@nostr-dev-kit/ndk";
     2	import type { Hexpubkey, NDKEvent, NDKKind, NDKSigner, NDKSubscription } from "@nostr-dev-kit/ndk";
     3	
     4	/**
     5	 * User session data
     6	 */
     7	export interface NDKSession {
     8	    pubkey: Hexpubkey;
     9	    followSet?: Set<Hexpubkey>;
    10	
    11	    /**
    12	     * Muted IDs (from kind 10000)
    13	     * Map<id, type> where type is "p" for pubkey or "e" for event
    14	     */
    15	    muteSet?: Map<string, string>;
    16	
    17	    /**
    18	     * Muted words (from kind 10000 "word" tags)
    19	     */
    20	    mutedWords?: Set<string>;
    21	
    22	    /**
    23	     * Blocked relay URLs (from kind 10001)
    24	     */
    25	    blockedRelays?: Set<string>;
    26	
    27	    /**
    28	     * User's relay list (from kind 10002)
    29	     */
    30	    relayList?: Map<string, { read: boolean; write: boolean }>;
    31	
    32	    /**
    33	     * Events that are unique per kind (replaceable events)
    34	     */
    35	    events: Map<NDKKind, NDKEvent | null>;
    36	
    37	    /**
    38	     * Active subscriptions for this session
    39	     */
    40	    subscriptions: NDKSubscription[];
    41	
    42	    /**
    43	     * Last time this session was active (seconds)
    44	     */
    45	    lastActive: number;
    46	
    47	    /**
    48	     * User preferences for this session
    49	     */
    50	    preferences?: SessionPreferences;
    51	}
    52	
    53	/**
    54	 * Event class constructor with static kinds property and from method
    55	 */
    56	export interface NDKEventConstructor {
    57	    kinds: NDKKind[];
    58	    from(event: NDKEvent): NDKEvent;
    59	}
    60	
    61	/**
    62	 * Monitor item - can be either an event constructor or a raw kind number
    63	 */
    64	export type MonitorItem = NDKEventConstructor | NDKKind;
    65	
    66	/**
    67	 * Options for starting a session
    68	 */
    69	export interface SessionStartOptions {
    70	    /**
    71	     * Fetch contacts (follows) - kind 3
    72	     */
    73	    follows?: boolean;
    74	
    75	    /**
    76	     * Fetch mute list (kind 10000)
    77	     */
    78	    mutes?: boolean;
    79	
    80	    /**
    81	     * Fetch blocked relay list (kind 10001)
    82	     */
    83	    blockedRelays?: boolean;
    84	
    85	    /**
    86	     * Fetch user's relay list (kind 10002)
    87	     */
    88	    relayList?: boolean;
    89	
    90	    /**
    91	     * Fetch NIP-60 wallet (kind 17375)
    92	     */
    93	    wallet?: boolean;
    94	
    95	    /**
    96	     * Monitor specific event kinds and/or event constructors
    97	     * @example
    98	     * ```typescript
    99	     * monitor: [NDKInterestList, 10050, 10051]
   100	     * // Monitors:
   101	     * // - NDKInterestList (wraps with constructor)
   102	     * // - Kind 10050 (as regular NDKEvent)
   103	     * // - Kind 10051 (as regular NDKEvent)
   104	     * ```
   105	     */
   106	    monitor?: MonitorItem[];
   107	
   108	    /**
   109	     * Set the muteFilter on NDK based on session's mute data
   110	     * @default true
   111	     */
   112	    setMuteFilter?: boolean;
   113	
   114	    /**
   115	     * Set the relayConnectionFilter on NDK based on session's blocked relays
   116	     * @default true
   117	     */
   118	    setRelayConnectionFilter?: boolean;
   119	}
   120	
   121	/**
   122	 * User preferences for a session
   123	 */
   124	export interface SessionPreferences {
   125	    /**
   126	     * Whether wallet fetching should be enabled for this session
   127	     */
   128	    walletEnabled?: boolean;
   129	}
   130	
   131	/**
   132	 * Serialized session data for storage
   133	 * Only persists identity and signer - all data comes from NDK cache
   134	 */
   135	export interface SerializedSession {
   136	    pubkey: Hexpubkey;
   137	    signerPayload?: string; // NDKSigner's toPayload() result
   138	    lastActive: number;
   139	    preferences?: SessionPreferences;
   140	}
   141	
   142	/**
   143	 * Session store state
   144	 */
   145	export interface SessionState {
   146	    ndk?: NDK;
   147	    sessions: Map<Hexpubkey, NDKSession>;
   148	    signers: Map<Hexpubkey, NDKSigner>;
   149	    activePubkey?: Hexpubkey;
   150	}
   151	
   152	/**
   153	 * Login options
   154	 */
   155	export interface LoginOptions extends SessionStartOptions {
   156	    /**
   157	     * Set this session as active immediately
   158	     */
   159	    setActive?: boolean;
   160	}

     1	import type NDK from "@nostr-dev-kit/ndk";
     2	import type { Hexpubkey, NDKSigner, NDKUser, NDKUserProfile } from "@nostr-dev-kit/ndk";
     3	import { NDKEvent, NDKKind, NDKPrivateKeySigner, NDKRelayList } from "@nostr-dev-kit/ndk";
     4	import { AuthManager } from "./auth-manager";
     5	import { PersistenceManager } from "./persistence-manager";
     6	import type { SessionStorage } from "./storage/types";
     7	import type { SessionStore } from "./store";
     8	import { createSessionStore } from "./store";
     9	import type { LoginOptions, NDKSession, SessionStartOptions } from "./types";
    10	import { debounce } from "./utils";
    11	
    12	export interface SessionManagerOptions {
    13	    /**
    14	     * Storage backend for persisting sessions
    15	     */
    16	    storage?: SessionStorage;
    17	
    18	    /**
    19	     * Automatically save sessions on state changes (default: true)
    20	     */
    21	    autoSave?: boolean;
    22	
    23	    /**
    24	     * Debounce time for auto-save in milliseconds (default: 500)
    25	     */
    26	    saveDebounceMs?: number;
    27	
    28	    /**
    29	     * What to fetch for all sessions (on login and restore)
    30	     */
    31	    fetches?: SessionStartOptions;
    32	}
    33	
    34	/**
    35	 * NDK Session Manager
    36	 *
    37	 * Manages user sessions with multi-account support and optional persistence.
    38	 * Refactored to follow Single Responsibility Principle with separate managers
    39	 * for authentication and persistence.
    40	 *
    41	 * @example
    42	 * ```typescript
    43	 * const ndk = new NDK({ explicitRelayUrls: ['wss://relay.damus.io'] });
    44	 * await ndk.connect();
    45	 *
    46	 * const sessions = new NDKSessionManager(ndk, {
    47	 *     fetches: { follows: true }
    48	 * });
    49	 * await sessions.login(signer);
    50	 *
    51	 * console.log('Active user:', sessions.activeUser);
    52	 * ```
    53	 */
    54	export class NDKSessionManager {
    55	    private store: ReturnType<typeof createSessionStore>;
    56	    private options: SessionManagerOptions;
    57	    private authManager: AuthManager;
    58	    private persistenceManager: PersistenceManager;
    59	    private unsubscribe?: () => void;
    60	
    61	    constructor(ndk: NDK, options: SessionManagerOptions = {}) {
    62	        this.store = createSessionStore();
    63	        this.options = {
    64	            autoSave: options.autoSave ?? true,
    65	            saveDebounceMs: options.saveDebounceMs ?? 500,
    66	            ...options,
    67	        };
    68	
    69	        // Initialize store with NDK
    70	        this.store.getState().init(ndk);
    71	
    72	        // Initialize managers
    73	        this.authManager = new AuthManager(this.store.getState(), () => this.store.getState());
    74	        this.persistenceManager = new PersistenceManager(this.options.storage, () => this.store.getState());
    75	
    76	        // Setup auto-save if enabled
    77	        if (this.options.autoSave && this.options.storage) {
    78	            this.setupAutoSave();
    79	        }
    80	    }
    81	
    82	    /**
    83	     * Get the current store state
    84	     */
    85	    getCurrentState() {
    86	        return this.store.getState();
    87	    }
    88	
    89	    /**
    90	     * Get all sessions
    91	     */
    92	    getSessions(): Map<Hexpubkey, NDKSession> {
    93	        return this.getCurrentState().sessions;
    94	    }
    95	
    96	    /**
    97	     * Get a specific session
    98	     */
    99	    getSession(pubkey: Hexpubkey): NDKSession | undefined {
   100	        return this.getCurrentState().sessions.get(pubkey);
   101	    }
   102	
   103	    /**
   104	     * Get the active session
   105	     */
   106	    get activeSession(): NDKSession | undefined {
   107	        const { activePubkey, sessions } = this.getCurrentState();
   108	        return activePubkey ? sessions.get(activePubkey) : undefined;
   109	    }
   110	
   111	    /**
   112	     * Get the active user
   113	     */
   114	    get activeUser(): NDKUser | undefined {
   115	        const session = this.activeSession;
   116	        const state = this.getCurrentState();
   117	        if (!session || !state.ndk) return undefined;
   118	
   119	        return state.ndk.getUser({ pubkey: session.pubkey });
   120	    }
   121	
   122	    /**
   123	     * Get the active pubkey
   124	     */
   125	    get activePubkey(): Hexpubkey | undefined {
   126	        return this.getCurrentState().activePubkey;
   127	    }
   128	
   129	    /**
   130	     * Check if a session is read-only (no signer available)
   131	     */
   132	    isReadOnly(pubkey?: Hexpubkey): boolean {
   133	        const targetPubkey = pubkey ?? this.getCurrentState().activePubkey;
   134	        if (!targetPubkey) return true;
   135	        return !this.getCurrentState().signers.has(targetPubkey);
   136	    }
   137	
   138	    /**
   139	     * Login with a signer or user
   140	     *
   141	     * @example
   142	     * ```typescript
   143	     * const signer = new NDKPrivateKeySigner(nsec);
   144	     * await sessions.login(signer);
   145	     * ```
   146	     */
   147	    async login(userOrSigner: NDKUser | NDKSigner, options: { setActive?: boolean } = {}): Promise<Hexpubkey> {
   148	        const loginOptions: LoginOptions = {
   149	            ...this.options.fetches,
   150	            setActive: options.setActive,
   151	        };
   152	        return this.authManager.login(userOrSigner, loginOptions);
   153	    }
   154	
   155	    /**
   156	     * Create a new account with optional profile, relays, wallet, and follows
   157	     *
   158	     * @param data - Account data to create
   159	     * @param data.profile - Optional profile metadata (kind:0)
   160	     * @param data.relays - Optional relay list for NIP-65 (kind:10002)
   161	     * @param data.wallet - Optional wallet configuration for NIP-60
   162	     * @param data.wallet.mints - Mint URLs for the wallet
   163	     * @param data.wallet.relays - Optional relays for wallet events
   164	     * @param data.follows - Optional list of pubkeys to follow (kind:3)
   165	     * @param opts - Behavior options
   166	     * @param opts.publish - Whether to publish events immediately (default: true)
   167	     * @param opts.signer - Optional signer to use instead of generating a new one
   168	     * @returns The signer and signed events array
   169	     *
   170	     * @example
   171	     * ```typescript
   172	     * // Publish immediately with generated signer
   173	     * const { signer, events } = await sessions.createAccount({
   174	     *   profile: { name: 'Alice', about: 'Hello Nostr!' },
   175	     *   relays: ['wss://relay.damus.io', 'wss://relay.primal.net'],
   176	     *   follows: ['pubkey1...', 'pubkey2...']
   177	     * });
   178	     *
   179	     * // Use existing signer
   180	     * const mySigner = NDKPrivateKeySigner.generate();
   181	     * const { signer, events } = await sessions.createAccount({
   182	     *   profile: { name: 'Alice', about: 'Hello Nostr!' }
   183	     * }, { signer: mySigner });
   184	     *
   185	     * // Get signed events without publishing
   186	     * const { signer, events } = await sessions.createAccount({
   187	     *   profile: { name: 'Alice', about: 'Hello Nostr!' },
   188	     *   relays: ['wss://relay.damus.io', 'wss://relay.primal.net']
   189	     * }, { publish: false });
   190	     * // events array will contain signed events when publish is false
   191	     * ```
   192	     */
   193	    async createAccount(
   194	        data?: {
   195	            profile?: NDKUserProfile;
   196	            relays?: string[];
   197	            wallet?: {
   198	                mints: string[];
   199	                relays?: string[];
   200	            };
   201	            follows?: Hexpubkey[];
   202	        },
   203	        opts?: {
   204	            publish?: boolean;
   205	            signer?: NDKPrivateKeySigner;
   206	        }
   207	    ): Promise<{ signer: NDKPrivateKeySigner; events: NDKEvent[] }> {
   208	        const state = this.getCurrentState();
   209	        const ndk = state.ndk;
   210	        if (!ndk) throw new Error("NDK not initialized");
   211	
   212	        const signer = opts?.signer ?? NDKPrivateKeySigner.generate();
   213	
   214	        // Only login if signer was not provided (i.e., we generated a new one)
   215	        if (!opts?.signer) {
   216	            await this.login(signer, { setActive: true });
   217	        }
   218	
   219	        const publish = opts?.publish !== false;
   220	        const events: NDKEvent[] = [];
   221	
   222	        if (data?.profile) {
   223	            const profileEvent = new NDKEvent(ndk, {
   224	                kind: NDKKind.Metadata,
   225	                content: JSON.stringify(data.profile),
   226	            });
   227	            await profileEvent.sign(signer);
   228	            if (publish) {
   229	                await profileEvent.publish();
   230	            } else {
   231	                events.push(profileEvent);
   232	            }
   233	        }
   234	
   235	        if (data?.relays && data.relays.length > 0) {
   236	            const relayList = new NDKRelayList(ndk);
   237	            relayList.bothRelayUrls = data.relays;
   238	            await relayList.sign(signer);
   239	            if (publish) {
   240	                await relayList.publish();
   241	            } else {
   242	                events.push(relayList);
   243	            }
   244	        }
   245	
   246	        if (data?.wallet) {
   247	            const { NDKCashuWallet } = await import("@nostr-dev-kit/wallet");
   248	            await NDKCashuWallet.create(ndk, data.wallet.mints, data.wallet.relays);
   249	        }
   250	
   251	        if (data?.follows && data.follows.length > 0) {
   252	            const contactList = new NDKEvent(ndk, {
   253	                kind: NDKKind.Contacts,
   254	                tags: data.follows.map((pubkey) => ["p", pubkey]),
   255	                content: "",
   256	            });
   257	            await contactList.sign(signer);
   258	            if (publish) {
   259	                await contactList.publish();
   260	            } else {
   261	                events.push(contactList);
   262	            }
   263	        }
   264	
   265	        return { signer, events };
   266	    }
   267	
   268	    /**
   269	     * Logout (remove) a session
   270	     *
   271	     * @param pubkey - Session to logout. If not provided, logs out active session.
   272	     */
   273	    logout(pubkey?: Hexpubkey): void {
   274	        this.authManager.logout(pubkey);
   275	    }
   276	
   277	    /**
   278	     * Switch to a different session
   279	     */
   280	    async switchTo(pubkey: Hexpubkey | null): Promise<void> {
   281	        await this.authManager.switchTo(pubkey);
   282	    }
   283	
   284	    /**
   285	     * Start fetching data for a session
   286	     */
   287	    startSession(pubkey: Hexpubkey, options: SessionStartOptions): void {
   288	        this.getCurrentState().startSession(pubkey, options);
   289	    }
   290	
   291	    /**
   292	     * Stop fetching data for a session
   293	     */
   294	    stopSession(pubkey: Hexpubkey): void {
   295	        this.getCurrentState().stopSession(pubkey);
   296	    }
   297	
   298	    /**
   299	     * Add monitors to the active session
   300	     *
   301	     * @example
   302	     * ```typescript
   303	     * // Add monitors to active session
   304	     * sessions.addMonitor([NDKInterestList, 10050, 10051]);
   305	     * ```
   306	     */
   307	    addMonitor(monitor: import("./types").MonitorItem[]): void {
   308	        this.getCurrentState().addMonitor(monitor);
   309	    }
   310	
   311	    /**
   312	     * Enable wallet fetching for a session
   313	     *
   314	     * @param pubkey - Session to enable wallet for. If not provided, uses active session.
   315	     *
   316	     * @example
   317	     * ```typescript
   318	     * // User wants to use wallet features
   319	     * sessions.enableWallet(userPubkey);
   320	     * ```
   321	     */
   322	    enableWallet(pubkey?: Hexpubkey): void {
   323	        const targetPubkey = pubkey ?? this.getCurrentState().activePubkey;
   324	        if (!targetPubkey) return;
   325	
   326	        const state = this.getCurrentState();
   327	        const session = state.sessions.get(targetPubkey);
   328	        if (!session) return;
   329	
   330	        // Update preference
   331	        state.updatePreferences(targetPubkey, { walletEnabled: true });
   332	
   333	        // If wallet is not already being fetched, restart the session with wallet enabled
   334	        const currentSubscriptions = session.subscriptions;
   335	        if (currentSubscriptions && currentSubscriptions.length > 0) {
   336	            // Stop current subscriptions and restart with wallet
   337	            state.stopSession(targetPubkey);
   338	            state.startSession(targetPubkey, { ...this.options.fetches, wallet: true });
   339	        } else {
   340	            // No active subscriptions, just update preference
   341	            // Will be used when session starts
   342	        }
   343	    }
   344	
   345	    /**
   346	     * Disable wallet fetching for a session
   347	     *
   348	     * @param pubkey - Session to disable wallet for. If not provided, uses active session.
   349	     *
   350	     * @example
   351	     * ```typescript
   352	     * // User wants to disable wallet features
   353	     * sessions.disableWallet(userPubkey);
   354	     * ```
   355	     */
   356	    disableWallet(pubkey?: Hexpubkey): void {
   357	        const targetPubkey = pubkey ?? this.getCurrentState().activePubkey;
   358	        if (!targetPubkey) return;
   359	
   360	        const state = this.getCurrentState();
   361	        const session = state.sessions.get(targetPubkey);
   362	        if (!session) return;
   363	
   364	        // Update preference
   365	        state.updatePreferences(targetPubkey, { walletEnabled: false });
   366	
   367	        // If session is running, restart without wallet
   368	        const currentSubscriptions = session.subscriptions;
   369	        if (currentSubscriptions && currentSubscriptions.length > 0) {
   370	            state.stopSession(targetPubkey);
   371	            state.startSession(targetPubkey, { ...this.options.fetches, wallet: false });
   372	        }
   373	    }
   374	
   375	    /**
   376	     * Check if wallet fetching is enabled for a session
   377	     *
   378	     * @param pubkey - Session to check. If not provided, uses active session.
   379	     * @returns true if wallet fetching is enabled
   380	     *
   381	     * @example
   382	     * ```typescript
   383	     * if (!sessions.isWalletEnabled()) {
   384	     *   // Show UI prompt to enable wallet
   385	     * }
   386	     * ```
   387	     */
   388	    isWalletEnabled(pubkey?: Hexpubkey): boolean {
   389	        const targetPubkey = pubkey ?? this.getCurrentState().activePubkey;
   390	        if (!targetPubkey) return false;
   391	
   392	        const session = this.getCurrentState().sessions.get(targetPubkey);
   393	        return session?.preferences?.walletEnabled ?? false;
   394	    }
   395	
   396	    /**
   397	     * Subscribe to state changes
   398	     *
   399	     * @example
   400	     * ```typescript
   401	     * const unsubscribe = sessions.subscribe((state) => {
   402	     *   console.log('Active pubkey:', state.activePubkey);
   403	     * });
   404	     * ```
   405	     */
   406	    subscribe(callback: (state: SessionStore) => void): () => void {
   407	        return this.store.subscribe(callback);
   408	    }
   409	
   410	    /**
   411	     * Restore sessions from storage
   412	     */
   413	    async restore(): Promise<void> {
   414	        await this.persistenceManager.restore();
   415	
   416	        // Start fetching data for all restored sessions
   417	        if (this.options.fetches) {
   418	            const state = this.getCurrentState();
   419	            for (const pubkey of state.sessions.keys()) {
   420	                const session = state.sessions.get(pubkey);
   421	                if (!session) continue;
   422	
   423	                // Respect saved wallet preference, or fall back to default
   424	                const walletEnabled = session.preferences?.walletEnabled ?? this.options.fetches.wallet ?? false;
   425	
   426	                const fetches = {
   427	                    ...this.options.fetches,
   428	                    wallet: walletEnabled,
   429	                };
   430	
   431	                state.startSession(pubkey, fetches);
   432	            }
   433	        }
   434	    }
   435	
   436	    /**
   437	     * Persist sessions to storage
   438	     */
   439	    async persist(): Promise<void> {
   440	        return this.persistenceManager.persist();
   441	    }
   442	
   443	    /**
   444	     * Clear all sessions from storage
   445	     */
   446	    async clear(): Promise<void> {
   447	        return this.persistenceManager.clear();
   448	    }
   449	
   450	    /**
   451	     * Cleanup and stop all subscriptions
   452	     */
   453	    destroy(): void {
   454	        const state = this.getCurrentState();
   455	
   456	        // Stop all session subscriptions
   457	        for (const pubkey of state.sessions.keys()) {
   458	            state.stopSession(pubkey);
   459	        }
   460	
   461	        // Unsubscribe from state changes
   462	        if (this.unsubscribe) {
   463	            this.unsubscribe();
   464	        }
   465	    }
   466	
   467	    private setupAutoSave(): void {
   468	        const debouncedPersist = debounce(() => {
   469	            this.persistenceManager.persist().catch((error) => {
   470	                console.error("Failed to auto-save sessions:", error);
   471	            });
   472	        }, this.options.saveDebounceMs!);
   473	
   474	        this.unsubscribe = this.store.subscribe(() => {
   475	            debouncedPersist();
   476	        });
   477	    }
   478	}
