 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# NMP M6 Sessions + Signers — Synthesis & Recommendation
     2	
     3	> Reads: `ndk-sessions.md`, `applesauce-sessions.md`, existing `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md`, NMP doctrine D0–D8 (`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5), `docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md`, current Rust shape in `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/identity.rs`.
     4	
     5	## 1. The nine questions, answered
     6	
     7	### 1.1 Signer trait shape
     8	
     9	**Recommendation:** keep current `IdentityModule::sign(ctx, id, &UnsignedEvent) -> BoxFuture<Result<SignedEvent, SigningError>>` (`identity.rs:18-22`). Add three things:
    10	
    11	a. **Encryption namespace** — mirror applesauce's optional capability pattern (`interop.ts:8-15`):
    12	
    13	```rust
    14	fn nip04(ctx: &IdentityContext, id: &IdentityId) -> Option<Box<dyn Nip04Capability>>;
    15	fn nip44(ctx: &IdentityContext, id: &IdentityId) -> Option<Box<dyn Nip44Capability>>;
    16	```
    17	
    18	Returning `Option` means readonly / extension-without-nip04 accounts don't have to throw at call time.
    19	
    20	b. **Mandatory post-conditions in the wrapper**, NOT in each implementation. Per applesauce's `account.ts:115-128`:
    21	
    22	```rust
    23	async fn sign_with_guards(...) -> Result<SignedEvent, SigningError> {
    24	    let precomputed_id = compute_event_id(&unsigned);
    25	    let signed = T::sign(ctx, id, &unsigned).await?;
    26	    if signed.id != precomputed_id { return Err(SigningError::SignerModifiedEvent); }
    27	    if signed.unsigned.pubkey != account_pubkey { return Err(SigningError::SignerPubkeyMismatch); }
    28	    Ok(signed)
    29	}
    30	```
    31	
    32	c. **Sync vs async:** all methods async (return `BoxFuture`). NDK's sync `pubkey` getter that can `throw "Not ready"` violates D6 — never replicate.
    33	
    34	**Errors:** extend `IdentityError` (currently 2 variants, `identity.rs:50-53`) with `Timeout(operation, ms)`, `SignerPubkeyMismatch`, `SignerModifiedEvent`, `NotReady`, `Unsupported(method)`. Per D6, these become toast state at the FFI; never `panic!`.
    35	
    36	### 1.2 Account/Session type — fields, mutable vs immutable
    37	
    38	NDK's `NDKSession` (`sessions/src/types.ts:7-51`) mixes identity, derived state, and live subscription handles. Applesauce's `IAccount` (`accounts/src/types.ts:26-42`) is just identity + signer + metadata.
    39	
    40	**Recommendation:** **adopt applesauce's narrow account shape; derived state lives in domain stores.**
    41	
    42	```rust
    43	// Persistent / serializable:
    44	pub struct AccountRecord<D: IdentityDescriptor> {
    45	    pub id: AccountId,           // ULID; distinct from pubkey (supports multiple accounts for one pubkey)
    46	    pub pubkey: Hexpubkey,
    47	    pub namespace: &'static str, // IdentityModule::NAMESPACE
    48	    pub descriptor: D,           // module-specific
    49	    pub display_name: Option<String>,
    50	    pub last_active: u64,
    51	    pub created_at: u64,
    52	}
    53	
    54	// Session-only (in-memory, projected into AppState.session):
    55	pub struct ActiveAccount {
    56	    pub id: AccountId,
    57	    pub pubkey: Hexpubkey,
    58	    pub capabilities: SignerCapabilities, // {can_sign, can_nip04, can_nip44, is_readonly}
    59	    pub display_name: Option<String>,
    60	}
    61	```
    62	
    63	`AccountId` (ULID) distinct from `pubkey` — required because applesauce allows multiple accounts for one pubkey (`manager.ts:60-63`), and NMP must support "same nsec, different relay policy" or "same bunker user from two devices."
    64	
    65	**Mutable vs immutable:**
    66	- `AccountRecord.pubkey`, `id`, `namespace` are immutable post-creation.
    67	- `descriptor`, `display_name`, `last_active` are mutable via dedicated actor messages.
    68	- Follow set, mute list, relay list **are not on the account** — query the domain stores by `pubkey`.
    69	
    70	### 1.3 AccountManager API
    71	
    72	NDK's `NDKSessionManager` (manager.ts) and applesauce's `AccountManager` (manager.ts) converge on the same surface. NMP's equivalent is the actor; the public API is the `AppAction` variants in `docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:104-110`:
    73	
    74	```rust
    75	AddAccountPrivateKey { nsec_or_ncryptsec: String, passphrase: Option<String> },
    76	AddAccountBunker { connect_uri: String },
    77	AddAccountExternal { kind: ExternalSignerKind },
    78	ActivateAccount { pubkey: String },
    79	RemoveAccount { pubkey: String, wipe: bool },
    80	```
    81	
    82	**Recommendation:** these five intents are the right primitives. Add three more for M6 completeness:
    83	
    84	```rust
    85	GenerateNewAccount { display_name: Option<String>, passphrase: String }, // create + nip-49 encrypt + publish kind:0/10002 if requested
    86	ListAccounts,                                                            // returns Vec<AccountRecord> in AppState
    87	RenameAccount { pubkey: String, display_name: String },                  // touches metadata only
    88	```
    89	
    90	`ListAccounts` is implicit (always in `AppState.sessions.accounts`); `RenameAccount` is metadata-only and writes through `KeyringCapability` (since the keyring already holds the account record).
    91	
    92	**Enumeration**: read-only via `AppState.session.accounts: Vec<AccountRecord>`. Reactive: the actor emits `AppUpdate::FullState` (or a `SessionDelta` variant) on add/remove/activate.
    93	
    94	**Switch order invariant** (from NDK `store.ts:279-329`): on `ActivateAccount`, the actor must (1) bind the signer, (2) rebuild mute/relay filters, (3) update active_pubkey, (4) emit ONE `AppUpdate::FullState`. Never partial updates.
    95	
    96	### 1.4 Reactivity model
    97	
    98	NDK uses zustand subscribe (`manager.ts:406`); applesauce uses rxjs `BehaviorSubject` (`manager.ts:10-15`). Both expose a reactive primitive directly to consumers.
    99	
   100	**Recommendation:** **neither.** NMP's reactivity is the `AppUpdate` stream over UniFFI (`api-surface.md:170-183`). Views subscribe to the stream; the active account is a field in `AppState.session`. There is no separate `manager.active$` observable. This matches D8's "snapshots by default."
   101	
   102	Per-view reactivity (e.g., a SessionSwitcher view) reads `AppState.session.accounts` and `AppState.session.active_pubkey`; re-renders when those change. The framework handles the diff; the view code is declarative.
   103	
   104	### 1.5 Persistence boundary
   105	
   106	NDK: persists `{pubkey, signerPayload, lastActive, preferences}` per session + global `activePubkey` (`types.ts:135-140` + `local-storage.ts:13-26`). Everything else derives from the cache.
   107	
   108	Applesauce: persists `{id, type, pubkey, signer, metadata}` per account (`types.ts:12-23`). No active marker. No derived state.
   109	
   110	**Recommendation for NMP:**
   111	
   112	| Lives in LMDB (`nmp-core` event store / domain stores) | Lives in `KeyringCapability` | In-memory only |
   113	| --- | --- | --- |
   114	| All received events (kind:0, 3, 10000, 10001, 10002, etc.) | Encrypted `AccountRecord` per account (one keyring entry per `AccountId`) | `ActiveAccount` (derived on activate) |
   115	| `FollowSet`, `MuteSet`, `RelayList` per pubkey (derived domain stores) | Encrypted bunker `local_signer` private keys (with the bunker descriptor) | Subscription handles per active account |
   116	| Last-active timestamp per account (so resume picks "most recent") | NIP-49-encrypted nsec bytes | Action ledger entries (already specified in M6) |
   117	
   118	Two things go in the keyring, NOT LMDB: (a) encrypted nsec for human accounts, (b) the NIP-46 `local_signer` private key per bunker connection. The keyring blob is opaque to `KeyringCapability` per `api-surface.md:198-203`; NMP serializes the `AccountRecord` (including the bunker `local_signer.private_key`) into bytes, then asks the keyring to store them.
   119	
   120	LMDB stores the *index* — `AccountId → keyring_key` — plus the public account record (display name, namespace, pubkey, last_active, created_at). The keyring stores the secret material.
   121	
   122	**Why split:** the keyring's threat model (passcode-gated, hardware-backed where available) is different from LMDB's. LMDB is on-disk-encrypted at the OS level only.
   123	
   124	### 1.6 kind:3 + kind:10002 auto-rewire
   125	
   126	**NDK's mechanism** (`sessions/src/store.ts:417-444` + `:449-487`): on session start, subscribe to a fixed kind set for the authoring pubkey; per-kind handlers dispatch on `event.kind`, drop older-`created_at` events, rebuild derived sets, store the raw event.
   127	
   128	**Applesauce's mechanism**: not implemented in the accounts package — the consumer subscribes via the EventStore and gets reactive results via rxjs query results.
   129	
   130	**NMP's preferred mechanism**: a kernel-owned **account-bootstrap planner** that, on `ActivateAccount`, opens a fixed subscription `{kinds: [0, 3, 10000, 10001, 10002], authors: [pubkey]}` against the user's outbox relays (or fallback). Per-kind event handlers update the corresponding domain store (`ProfileStore`, `FollowSetStore`, `MuteSetStore`, `RelayListStore`). The subscription is owned by the account session and torn down on `RemoveAccount`/`ActivateAccount(other)`.
   131	
   132	The per-kind handler logic is mechanical (NDK got it right at `store.ts:492-611`); port the algorithm but write the data into domain stores instead of session fields. **D4 invariant:** the `FollowSetStore` is the sole writer of the `FollowSet`; views read from it.
   133	
   134	### 1.7 bunker:// flow
   135	
   136	NDK's parser (`core/src/signers/nip46/index.ts:204-215`) and applesauce's parser (`helpers/nostr-connect.ts:86-98`) agree on the URL shape. Applesauce additionally validates `isHexKey(remote)`.
   137	
   138	**Recommendation:** implement in `nmp-core` (not in a separate module) since the URL format is part of the kernel-facing `IdentityDescriptor`:
   139	
   140	```rust
   141	pub struct BunkerUri {
   142	    pub remote_pubkey: [u8; 32],  // validated hex
   143	    pub relays: Vec<String>,       // ≥1
   144	    pub secret: Option<String>,
   145	}
   146	
   147	impl BunkerUri {
   148	    pub fn parse(uri: &str) -> Result<Self, IdentityError> { /* ... */ }
   149	}
   150	```
   151	
   152	RPC setup mirrors `nip46/index.ts:315-368`: open a long-lived subscription on `{kinds: [24133], "#p": [local_signer.pubkey]}`, send `connect [user_pubkey, secret?]` request, await `"ack"`, issue `get_public_key`, then optional `switch_relays` with 5s timeout (`index.ts:375-411`).
   153	
   154	**Reconnect strategy:** on app restart, re-deserialize from keyring (which includes `local_signer.private_key` + `relay_urls` + `bunker_pubkey` + `user_pubkey`), re-open the subscription, but **do NOT re-issue `connect`** — the bunker already trusts this local signer (`index.ts:592-607`). This is the key insight: bunker pairing is per-local-signer-key, not per-session.
   155	
   156	**On websocket churn:** the subscription must auto-reconnect (NDK uses NDK's normal pool retry; applesauce uses `repeat() + retry()`, `nostr-connect-signer.ts:153-161`). NMP's relay pool already retries; the NIP-46 client should ride that machinery.
   157	
   158	**Secret parameter handling:** the secret is **only** valid for the initial `connect` call; it is NOT a long-term token. After ack, the bunker authorizes by `local_signer.pubkey`. The secret can be persisted in `BunkerUri.secret` (some bunkers re-validate on every reconnect; AlbyHub does this) — NDK persists it (`nip46/index.ts:547`), applesauce does too (`nostr-connect-account.ts:24-29`). **NMP: persist it.**
   159	
   160	### 1.8 NIP-07 wasm wrapping
   161	
   162	NDK (`core/src/signers/nip07/index.ts`): polls for `window.nostr` with bounded timeout (`waitForExtension`, line 222-246); queues encryption calls and retries on "call already executing" (line 169-220); `toPayload` is `{"type":"nip07","payload":""}` (line 253-258).
   163	
   164	Applesauce (`packages/signers/src/signers/extension-signer.ts`, 37 LOC): caches pubkey after first call (line 17); no queue at the signer level (uses account-level queue).
   165	
   166	**Recommendation for NMP** (deferred to M15 cross-platform, not M6 critical path):
   167	
   168	- Wasm-side bridge exposes a `WebSignerCapability` (analog to `ExternalSignerCapability` in `api-surface.md:212-215`) that calls `window.nostr.*` from a JS shim.
   169	- The Rust side treats this exactly like `IdentityScopeKind::ExternalSigner` — no special case.
   170	- Pubkey caching happens at the wasm bridge layer (one round-trip per session).
   171	- Encryption serialization happens at the per-account queue (actor inbox), not in the bridge.
   172	- Timeout: 5s for any single call; surface `IdentityError::Timeout("extension call", 5000)`.
   173	
   174	**Not in M6 scope.** Web is M15. But mention the parallel here so the M6 design doesn't accidentally close the door.
   175	
   176	### 1.9 Multi-account UX surface for M6 vs deferred
   177	
   178	M6 deliverables per `docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:11-19`:
   179	
   180	| In scope for M6 | Deferred (M7+) |
   181	| --- | --- |
   182	| Paste nsec (raw + ncryptsec) | `nostrconnect://` (client-initiated reverse handshake) |
   183	| Generate new nsec + NIP-49 encrypt + keyring store | Multi-account switcher UI (M8 per `docs/plan/m8-multi-account.md`) |
   184	| Paste `bunker://` URL → live signer | Hardware signers (Coldcard, Krux, etc.) — analogous to applesauce's SerialPortSigner |
   185	| `KeychainCapability` real impl on iOS | NIP-07 web extension (M15) |
   186	| Active account binding (one at a time) | Account metadata (color, display name editing) |
   187	| Action ledger w/ atomic publish + store | Account-rotation / signer migration UX |
   188	| Compose flow on iOS | NIP-55 / Amber-style iOS-app deeplink signers |
   189	
   190	**Critical M6 invariant:** one active account at a time is fine. Multi-account *visibility* (showing other accounts in a switcher) is M8. **But** the data model must already support multiple accounts in the keyring + LMDB so M8 is purely a UI/UX milestone — don't bake "single account" into any schema or actor message.
   191	
   192	## 2. Side-by-side scorecard
   193	
   194	| Dimension | NDK | applesauce | NMP recommendation |
   195	| --- | --- | --- | --- |
   196	| Signer trait size | 9 required + 1 optional method | 2 required + 2 optional namespaces | 3 required + 2 optional (matches applesauce) |
   197	| Return shape for sign | sig string only | full signed event | full signed event (already in `identity.rs:65-69`) |
   198	| Sync `pubkey` accessor | yes, throws if not ready | no, async only | async only |
   199	| Type-tagged signer registry | yes (`registry.ts:7-14`) | yes (`manager.ts:33-42`) | `IdentityModule::NAMESPACE` (already in `identity.rs:9`) |
   200	| Per-account queue | no (NIP-07 has its own encrypt queue) | yes, mandatory (`account.ts:137-187`) | yes, per-account actor inbox serializes |
   201	| Signer-mismatch post-conditions | no | yes (`account.ts:115-128`) | **yes, mandatory** — add to NMP wrapper |
   202	| Bunker URL parser | regex hostname/pathname (no hex check) | URL + isHexKey validation | strict hex validation |
   203	| `switch_relays` NIP-46 ext | yes, 5s timeout (`nip46/index.ts:375-411`) | yes, async retry (`nostr-connect-signer.ts:413-430`) | yes, 5s timeout |
   204	| Reactive active-account | zustand store subscription | rxjs BehaviorSubject | `AppUpdate::FullState`/`SessionDelta` only |
   205	| Persist active marker | yes | no | yes (UX-friendly) |
   206	| Auto-save | debounced 500ms | none (explicit `toJSON`) | debounced via actor tick |
   207	| Derived state location | on session struct | not in account package | own domain stores |
   208	| Storage adapter trait | yes (`SessionStorage`) | no | `KeyringCapability` + LMDB are NMP's "adapters" |
   209	| `replaceAccount` (signer rotation) | logout+login | explicit method | dedicated `RotateAccountSigner` action; aborts in-flight |
   210	| Read-only fallback on signer-restore-fail | yes (`persistence-manager.ts:75-88`) | implicit via ReadonlySigner | yes, with toast (D6) |
   211	
   212	## 3. Doctrine sanity-check
   213	
   214	| Doctrine | Risk vector | Mitigation |
   215	| --- | --- | --- |
   216	| D0 (no app nouns in kernel) | "HumanAccount" / "Bunker" naming risks app-coupling | These are `IdentityModule` namespaces, not core enum variants. The kernel only knows `IdentityScopeKind::{HumanAccount, AppLocal, ExternalSigner, Ephemeral}` (`identity.rs:27-32`) — that's the right abstraction level. |
   217	| D1 (best-effort rendering) | Account display-name takes a moment to load on switch | `ActiveAccount.display_name: Option<String>` is a placeholder field; view payload renders pubkey-truncation until loaded. |
   218	| D2 (negentropy first) | Account-bootstrap subscriptions are REQ-based | First fetch of kind:0/3/10002 is REQ; subsequent reconciliation can use NIP-77 (M7+). |
   219	| D3 (outbox auto) | Bootstrap subscription needs to know the user's outbox before having read kind:10002 | Bootstrap goes to default-relay list on first activation; rewires to outbox once kind:10002 lands. **Same idempotent re-compile pattern as views.** |
   220	| D4 (single writer per fact) | NDK's session struct holds derived state | NMP splits: domain stores own derived state; account record owns identity only. |
   221	| D5 (snapshots bounded by what's open) | Per-account `walletEnabled` opt-in is the right shape | NDK already does this — copy verbatim (`sessions/src/manager.ts:322-394`). |
   222	| D6 (errors never cross FFI) | `IdentityError` is too narrow today | Extend per §1.1. Every error becomes a toast field. |
   223	| D7 (capabilities report) | `KeychainCapability::store` is one of the most "decision-shaped" capabilities | The capability stores opaque bytes; NMP decides what to encrypt and what key derivation to use. Per `api-surface.md:198-203` this is already correct. |
   224	| D8 (reactivity contract) | Account-bootstrap subscriptions emit derived-store deltas | Per-domain-store reverse-index participation is the existing pattern — account bootstrap is just another producer. ≤60Hz cap applies uniformly. |
   225	
   226	## 4. Files-to-touch checklist for #43
   227	
   228	If acting on this synthesis, the M6 impl agent's files-to-touch is approximately:
   229	
   230	- `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/identity.rs` — extend `IdentityError`; add `Nip04Capability`/`Nip44Capability` optional traits; add signer-wrapper with post-conditions.
   231	- `crates/nmp-core/src/identity/{registry,bunker_uri,nip49}.rs` (new) — type-tagged dispatch, bunker URL parser, NIP-49 encrypt/decrypt helpers.
   232	- `crates/nmp-modules/nmp-identity-human/` (new) — `HumanAccount` module (nsec + ncryptsec).
   233	- `crates/nmp-modules/nmp-identity-bunker/` (new) — NIP-46 client implementation; rides relay pool.
   234	- `crates/nmp-core/src/keyring/` — `KeyringCapability` integration with account-record serde + opaque blob.
   235	- `crates/nmp-core/src/app/state.rs` — add `session: SessionState { accounts: Vec<AccountRecord>, active_pubkey: Option<Hexpubkey> }`.
   236	- `crates/nmp-core/src/actions/account.rs` (new) — handlers for `AddAccount*`, `ActivateAccount`, `RemoveAccount`, `GenerateNewAccount`.
   237	- iOS side: implement `KeyringCapability` for real (Security framework, app-private access group); login screen with three flows.
   238	
   239	## 5. Single biggest risk to avoid
   240	
   241	**The biggest mistake NMP can make is treating "session" and "account" as separate concepts.** Both NDK (`NDKSession`) and the React-side store create a per-pubkey blob that holds *both* identity and derived state, then bolt on a separate signer map. This makes activation atomicity hard (NDK explicitly comments on race conditions at `store.ts:279-281`).
   242	
   243	NMP's actor model gives this for free: one `AccountId` is the unit of everything. The "session" is just "this account is currently active" — a single `Option<AccountId>` in `AppState`. Derived state lives in domain stores keyed by pubkey, queryable for any account whether active or not.
   244	
   245	Adopt applesauce's `IAccount` as the mental model (`packages/accounts/src/types.ts:26-42`), not NDK's `NDKSession`. The fewer fields on the account record, the smaller the surface for race conditions.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Applesauce Accounts + Signers — Deep Dive
     2	
     3	> Source: `/private/tmp/nostr-research/applesauce`. Applesauce uses the term **"accounts"** for what NDK calls "sessions"; the multi-account container is `AccountManager`. The wire-level signer abstraction is `ISigner`. See `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md` (already in repo) for the signer-only deep-dive; this doc covers the account/session layer + delta against the existing signer doc.
     4	
     5	## 1. Package layout
     6	
     7	- `packages/signers/src/interop.ts` (82 LOC) — the `ISigner` trait + connection-method plumbing.
     8	- `packages/signers/src/signers/*` — 10 signer implementations (8 production + 2 deprecated/aliases). Per-signer LOC table in `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md:24-33`.
     9	- `packages/accounts/src/account.ts` (188 LOC) — `BaseAccount` with the load-bearing per-account queue and signer-mismatch guards.
    10	- `packages/accounts/src/manager.ts` (193 LOC) — `AccountManager` with rxjs `BehaviorSubject` for reactive active-account.
    11	- `packages/accounts/src/proxy-signer.ts` (37 LOC) — the rxjs proxy that makes `manager.signer` stable across active-account swaps.
    12	- `packages/accounts/src/accounts/*` — 9 account adapters (Extension, Password, Readonly, PrivateKey, NostrConnect, AmberClipboard, AndroidNative, SerialPort, Simple).
    13	
    14	**No "session" concept** in applesauce. The library deliberately does NOT track per-account derived state (followSet, muteSet, etc.) — those live in the consumer's `EventStore` and are queried per-render. This is the cleanest separation in the JS ecosystem.
    15	
    16	## 2. `ISigner` (`packages/signers/src/interop.ts:5-16`)
    17	
    18	```ts
    19	export type ISigner = {
    20	  getPublicKey: () => Promise<string>;
    21	  signEvent: (template: EventTemplate) => Promise<NostrEvent>;
    22	  nip04?: { encrypt(pubkey, plaintext): Promise<string>; decrypt(pubkey, ciphertext): Promise<string> };
    23	  nip44?: { encrypt(pubkey, plaintext): Promise<string>; decrypt(pubkey, ciphertext): Promise<string> };
    24	};
    25	```
    26	
    27	Four methods total. Differences from NDK's `NDKSigner`:
    28	- `getPublicKey` is async (NDK has both sync `pubkey` and async `user()`).
    29	- `signEvent` returns the **full signed `NostrEvent`** (NDK returns only the sig).
    30	- `nip04`/`nip44` are **optional namespaces**, not required methods — extension/readonly signers can lack them entirely without any "Not supported" stubs.
    31	- **No `toPayload` on the signer.** Serialization is the account's job (`account.toJSON()`), so signers stay storage-agnostic.
    32	
    33	**Applies to NMP:** the smaller surface is right. NMP's `IdentityModule::sign` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/identity.rs:18-22`) currently takes `&UnsignedEvent` and returns `Result<SignedEvent, SigningError>` — already closer to applesauce's "return the full event" than NDK's "return sig only". **Keep this.**
    34	
    35	**Applies to NMP:** the optional-namespace pattern for encryption (returning `Option<NipXxEncryptor>`) is what NMP's `IdentityModule` should grow into — different account types have genuinely different capability surfaces.
    36	
    37	## 3. `IAccount` (`packages/accounts/src/types.ts:26-42`)
    38	
    39	```ts
    40	export interface IAccount<Signer extends ISigner = ISigner, SignerData = any, Metadata = any> extends ISigner {
    41	  id: string;
    42	  name?: string;
    43	  pubkey: string;
    44	  metadata?: Metadata;
    45	  signer: Signer;
    46	  type: string;
    47	  disableQueue?: boolean;
    48	  toJSON(): SerializedAccount<SignerData, Metadata>;
    49	}
    50	```
    51	
    52	Three things this contract pins:
    53	- **`IAccount extends ISigner`**: the account IS the signer the rest of the app uses. There's no separate "give me the signer for this account" step — the account is the surface, and it wraps signer calls with queue+mismatch guards.
    54	- **`metadata: Metadata` (parameterized)**: per-account app-level state (display name, color, custom relays). The library doesn't touch it; the app does.
    55	- **`SerializedAccount`** (`types.ts:12-23`) is `{id, type, pubkey, signer, metadata?}` — explicitly typed by the account type's `SignerData` and `Metadata` generics.
    56	
    57	**Applies to NMP:** the "account wraps signer" pattern is the right shape for NMP's `IdentityModule::HumanAccount` etc. `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/identity.rs:11` has `Descriptor: Clone + Serialize + DeserializeOwned` — that's the analog of `IAccountConstructor.fromJSON` (line 51-53).
    58	
    59	## 4. `BaseAccount` — three load-bearing details (`packages/accounts/src/account.ts`)
    60	
    61	### 4.1 Per-account request queue (lines 137-187)
    62	
    63	```ts
    64	protected waitForQueue<T>(operation: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
    65	    if (this.disableQueue) return operation();
    66	    if (this.lock && this.abort) {
    67	        const p = wrapInSignal(
    68	            this.lock.then(() => { this.abort?.signal.throwIfAborted(); return operation(); }),
    69	            this.abort.signal,
    70	        );
    71	        this.lock = p.catch(() => {}).finally(this.reduceQueue.bind(this));
    72	        this.queueLength++;
    73	        return p;
    74	    } else {
    75	        // ... first-in-queue path ...
    76	    }
    77	}
    78	```
    79	
    80	Every privileged call (`getPublicKey`, `signEvent`, `nip04.*`, `nip44.*`) routes through `operation() → waitForQueue` (lines 86-89, 60-73, 67-73, 107-128). Reasons captured in `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md:114-119`:
    81	- Extension and NIP-46 signers cannot handle concurrent requests reliably.
    82	- User-facing prompts (password unlock, amber intent) need serialization.
    83	- `abortQueue(reason)` cancels everything in-flight on logout / signer swap.
    84	
    85	**Applies to NMP:** the actor model gives NMP serialization-for-free per account (one inbox per account-domain handler). The lesson is that **two concurrent SignerCapability calls for the same account must not race**; the actor must serialize them per-account, not globally.
    86	
    87	### 4.2 `SignerMismatchError` (lines 33, 109, 122-124)
    88	
    89	```ts
    90	signEvent(template: EventTemplate): Promise<NostrEvent> {
    91	    if (!Reflect.has(template, "pubkey")) Reflect.set(template, "pubkey", this.pubkey);
    92	    return this.operation(async () => {
    93	      const id = getEventHash(template as UnsignedEvent);
    94	      const result = await this.signer.signEvent(template);
    95	      if (result.pubkey !== this.pubkey) throw new SignerMismatchError("Signer signed with wrong pubkey");
    96	      if (result.id !== id) throw new SignerMismatchError("Signer modified event");
    97	      return result;
    98	    });
    99	}
   100	```
   101	
   102	**Three integrity checks**:
   103	1. Pre-compute the event hash; if the signer returns an event with a different `id`, the signer modified the template.
   104	2. The returned `pubkey` must match the account's stored pubkey.
   105	3. `getPublicKey` (line 106-112) similarly asserts the signer's pubkey matches the account's stored pubkey.
   106	
   107	**Applies to NMP:** **MUST copy.** A malicious or buggy signer (compromised extension, broken bunker, hardware glitch) could otherwise sign events as a *different* user without detection. NMP's `IdentityModule::sign` wrapper must enforce both checks before returning `Ok(SignedEvent)` — currently no such assertion exists in `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/identity.rs`.
   108	
   109	### 4.3 `metadata$` BehaviorSubject (lines 51-57)
   110	
   111	Per-account app-level state with rxjs reactivity. Not serialized by default — the app's `toJSON` override decides whether to persist metadata. **Applies to NMP:** NMP can support "account custom name / color / muted relays" the same way — per-account metadata is a domain-store concern, not a kernel concern. Keep it out of the `IdentityModule` trait surface.
   112	
   113	## 5. `AccountManager` (`packages/accounts/src/manager.ts`)
   114	
   115	### 5.1 Reactive active account (lines 10-15, 99-115)
   116	
   117	```ts
   118	active$ = new BehaviorSubject<IAccount<any, any, Metadata> | undefined>(undefined);
   119	accounts$ = new BehaviorSubject<IAccount<any, any, Metadata>[]>([]);
   120	// ...
   121	setActive(id: string | IAccount<any, any, Metadata>) {
   122	    const account = this.getAccount(id);
   123	    if (!account) throw new Error("Cant find account with that ID");
   124	    if (this.active$.value?.id !== account.id) {
   125	        this.active$.next(account);
   126	    }
   127	}
   128	```
   129	
   130	`active$` and `accounts$` are rxjs `BehaviorSubject`s — views subscribe and re-render on `.next(...)`. The de-duplication check (`!== account.id`) avoids redundant re-renders.
   131	
   132	**Applies to NMP:** rxjs maps mechanically to NMP's `AppUpdate` stream. The de-dup discipline matters: the actor should not emit a `FullState` if `active_pubkey` didn't actually change (`AppUpdate` deltas already cover this if implemented carefully).
   133	
   134	### 5.2 The `ProxySigner` pattern (`packages/accounts/src/proxy-signer.ts`)
   135	
   136	```ts
   137	export class ProxySigner<T extends ISigner> implements ISigner {
   138	    private _signer: T | undefined;
   139	    protected get signer(): T {
   140	        if (!this._signer) throw new Error(this.error || "Missing signer");
   141	        return this._signer;
   142	    }
   143	    constructor(protected upstream: Observable<T | undefined>, protected error?: string) {
   144	        this.upstream.subscribe((signer) => (this._signer = signer));
   145	    }
   146	    async signEvent(template: EventTemplate): Promise<NostrEvent> { return this.signer.signEvent(template); }
   147	    async getPublicKey(): Promise<string> { return this.signer.getPublicKey(); }
   148	    // ...
   149	}
   150	```
   151	
   152	`AccountManager` exposes `signer: ISigner` (line 21-28) which is a `ProxySigner` over `active$`. **What this enables:** consumers do `new EventFactory({ signer: manager.signer })` *once*, and the factory continues to "just work" when the user switches accounts — the proxy always re-dispatches to whatever is in `active$`. **No re-binding required.**
   153	
   154	**Applies to NMP:** this is exactly the problem NMP avoids by having the actor own the signer binding internally. Native code in NMP never holds a signer reference — it issues `AppAction::SendNote { … }` and the actor uses the currently-active account. **Confirms NMP's existing design.**
   155	
   156	### 5.3 Account-type registry (lines 33-42, 161-178)
   157	
   158	```ts
   159	registerType<S extends ISigner>(accountType: IAccountConstructor<S, any, Metadata>) {
   160	    if (!accountType.type) throw new Error(`Account class missing static "type" field`);
   161	    if (this.types.has(accountType.type)) throw new Error(`An account type of ${accountType.type} already exists`);
   162	    this.types.set(accountType.type, accountType);
   163	}
   164	// ...
   165	fromJSON(accounts: SerializedAccount<any, Metadata>[], quite = false) {
   166	    for (const json of accounts) {
   167	        const AccountType = this.types.get(json.type);
   168	        if (!AccountType) { if (!quite) throw new Error(`Missing account type ${json.type}`); else continue; }
   169	        const account = AccountType.fromJSON(json);
   170	        this.addAccount(account);
   171	    }
   172	}
   173	```
   174	
   175	Plus `registerCommonAccountTypes(manager)` (`accounts/common.ts:9-15`) which bulk-registers the 5 most-common types in one call.
   176	
   177	**Applies to NMP:** type-tagged dispatch on a static `type` string is the same pattern NDK uses (`core/src/signers/registry.ts:7-14`). NMP's existing `IdentityModule::NAMESPACE` const (`identity.rs:9`) is the analog — confirm that the orchestrator code path uses this for serde dispatch.
   178	
   179	### 5.4 `replaceAccount` (lines 87-95)
   180	
   181	```ts
   182	replaceAccount(old: string | IAccount, account: IAccount) {
   183	    this.addAccount(account);
   184	    const id = typeof account === "string" ? account : account.id;
   185	    if (this.active$.value?.id === id) this.setActive(account);
   186	    this.removeAccount(old);
   187	}
   188	```
   189	
   190	Caused the bug in commit `96120548` (cited in `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md:132`) — "ActionRunner cache causing actions to have wrong `user` when accounts change." **Applies to NMP:** signer-rotation (e.g., user reconnects a bunker with a new local key) must invalidate any in-flight actions bound to the old account. The actor must abort and re-route.
   191	
   192	## 6. Signer adapters — what's interesting
   193	
   194	### 6.1 `NostrConnectSigner` + `parseBunkerURI` (`packages/signers/src/signers/nostr-connect-signer.ts` + `helpers/nostr-connect.ts`)
   195	
   196	`helpers/nostr-connect.ts:86-98` shows the canonical bunker URI parser:
   197	
   198	```ts
   199	export function parseBunkerURI(uri: string): BunkerURI {
   200	  const url = new URL(uri);
   201	  // firefox puts pubkey part in host, chrome puts pubkey in pathname
   202	  const remote = url.host || url.pathname.replace("//", "");
   203	  if (!isHexKey(remote)) throw new Error("Invalid bunker URI: remote is not a valid hex key");
   204	  const relays = url.searchParams.getAll("relay");
   205	  if (relays.length === 0) throw new Error("Invalid bunker URI: missing relays");
   206	  const secret = url.searchParams.get("secret") ?? undefined;
   207	  return { remote, relays, secret };
   208	}
   209	```
   210	
   211	Three validations: `isHexKey(remote)`, at-least-one relay, secret is optional. NDK's parser (`core/src/signers/nip46/index.ts:204-215`) has the same hostname/pathname dance but does NOT validate isHex — **applesauce's version is stricter**.
   212	
   213	`NostrConnectSigner.fromBunkerURI(uri, opts)` (`nostr-connect-signer.ts:453-463`):
   214	
   215	```ts
   216	static async fromBunkerURI(uri, options?) {
   217	    const { remote, relays, secret } = NostrConnectSigner.parseBunkerURI(uri);
   218	    const client = new NostrConnectSigner({ relays, remote, ...options });
   219	    await client.connect(secret, options?.permissions);
   220	    return client;
   221	}
   222	```
   223	
   224	One static method, parse + construct + connect. **Applies to NMP:** the NIP-46 module should expose exactly this shape — `IdentityModule::ExternalSigner::create(IdentityDescriptor::BunkerUri(uri))` parses and dials, returning a fully-connected signer (or `IdentityError::InvalidDescriptor(...)`).
   225	
   226	### 6.2 Reconnect resilience (lines 145-161)
   227	
   228	```ts
   229	this.req = from(this.subscriptionMethod(this.relays, [{ kinds: [kinds.NostrConnect], "#p": [pubkey] }]))
   230	    .pipe(repeat(), retry(), filter((event) => typeof event !== "string"))
   231	    .subscribe(this.handleEvent.bind(this));
   232	```
   233	
   234	`repeat() + retry()` rxjs operators make the bunker subscription self-heal on websocket churn (commit `e6d5613b` per `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md:77`). **Applies to NMP:** NMP's NIP-46 client must reconnect transparently. The relay-pool subscription planner already does this for normal REQs; the NIP-46 client likely needs to ride that same machinery rather than open its own raw connection.
   235	
   236	### 6.3 `switchRelays` (lines 413-430) — protocol extension
   237	
   238	```ts
   239	async switchRelays(): Promise<string[] | null> {
   240	    await this.requireConnection();
   241	    const result = await this.makeRequest(NostrConnectMethod.SwitchRelays, []);
   242	    if (result !== null && Array.isArray(result) && result.length > 0) {
   243	        this.log("Switching relays from", this.relays, "to", result);
   244	        this.relays = result;
   245	        if (this.listening) { await this.close(); await this.open(); }
   246	    }
   247	    return result;
   248	}
   249	```
   250	
   251	Mirror of NDK's `switch_relays` (`core/src/signers/nip46/index.ts:375-411`). Both libraries support this NIP-46 extension; both tear down + rebuild the subscription. **Applies to NMP:** support it from day one — bunkers actively use it (nsec.app, AlbyHub).
   252	
   253	### 6.4 `nostrconnect://` URI (client-initiated) (lines 432-440)
   254	
   255	The reverse flow — the client publishes a `nostrconnect://` URI containing its local pubkey + secret + relays + metadata, and waits for any bunker to connect to it. Implementation in `helpers/nostr-connect.ts:156-167`. **Applies to NMP M6:** the spec mentions only `bunker://` (`docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:13`); `nostrconnect://` is an addition worth tracking but can be M7+ since the bunker-paste flow covers 95% of M6's exit criterion.
   256	
   257	### 6.5 Encryption queue *not* needed at signer level
   258	
   259	Unlike NDK, applesauce does NOT have a per-signer encryption queue in `nostr-connect-signer.ts`. Instead, the **account-level queue in `BaseAccount.operation()`** (account.ts:86-89, 60-73) covers it. One queue, not two. **Applies to NMP:** preserve the single-queue principle. The actor serializes per-account; signer impls can be lock-free.
   260	
   261	### 6.6 `AmberClipboardSigner` — clipboard IPC (`amber-clipboard-signer.ts`)
   262	
   263	Already covered in `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md:94-103`. Key takeaways: a `visibilitychange` listener with a 500ms guard against the immediate-fire race; `rejectPending` for canceling in-flight requests; the entire signer is unusable on non-Android (`SUPPORTED` check, line 12-17). **Applies to NMP:** any iOS-side Secure-Enclave / external-app signer must have the same "visible again → read response → settle pending promise" shape, with explicit cancellation on logout/switch.
   264	
   265	### 6.7 `AndroidNativeSigner` — Capacitor plugin (121 LOC)
   266	
   267	Covered in `docs/research/applesauce/signers.md:105-109`. Important detail (lines 67-96): pre-computes the event id before sending to the plugin (`getEventHash(withPubkey)`), so the plugin returns a correlation id that the signer can verify. **Applies to NMP:** NMP's signer wrapper should likewise pre-compute id so the post-condition check (§4.2) is meaningful.
   268	
   269	### 6.8 `ExtensionSigner` — pubkey caching (36 LOC)
   270	
   271	Lines 17, 22-28: pubkey cached after first `getPublicKey()` call (commit `0867a502`). The cache is **per-instance**, not per-pubkey — a fresh `new ExtensionSigner()` re-prompts. **Applies to NMP:** the wasm/web bridge (post-M15) should similarly cache; pubkey is `O(1)` to compute server-side but `O(user-attention)` if the extension prompts for confirmation.
   272	
   273	## 7. Persistence — applesauce vs NDK
   274	
   275	| Aspect | applesauce | NDK |
   276	| --- | --- | --- |
   277	| What's persisted | `{id, type, pubkey, signer: SignerData, metadata}` per account | `{pubkey, signerPayload, lastActive, preferences}` per session |
   278	| Active marker | NOT persisted by `AccountManager.toJSON()` (lines 143-155) | Persisted as `activePubkey` (`storage/local-storage.ts:19`) |
   279	| Derived state (followSet, mutes) | NOT persisted, NOT tracked by account layer | NOT persisted, but tracked in-memory by session (`types.ts:9-30`) |
   280	| Storage adapter abstraction | None — `toJSON()`/`fromJSON()`, caller writes | `SessionStorage` interface (`storage/types.ts:8-23`) |
   281	| Auto-save | None — explicit `manager.toJSON()` calls | Debounced 500ms auto-save (`manager.ts:467-477`) |
   282	
   283	**Applesauce's choice (no active marker)** is opinionated: the app re-prompts the user on every cold start. **NDK's choice (persist active)** is friendlier but means "active" can be stale across signer-rotation events. **Applies to NMP:** persist active pubkey per NDK; let the app config decide whether to re-prompt anyway (UX-level decision, not framework).
   284	
   285	## 8. NMP design implications (applesauce-specific)
   286	
   287	1. **`IAccount extends ISigner`** is the cleanest contract. NMP's `IdentityModule::sign` already takes `(ctx, id, unsigned)` — the account is implicit in `id`. Keep it.
   288	2. **Per-account queue is load-bearing.** Even if NMP's actor model serializes per-account naturally, document that the framework MUST never call a signer concurrently for the same account.
   289	3. **Signer-mismatch post-conditions** are mandatory. Add to `IdentityModule::sign` wrapper: assert returned `pubkey == account.pubkey` AND `id == precomputed_id`.
   290	4. **Type-tagged registry** — `IdentityModule::NAMESPACE` is already in place; ensure serde dispatch uses it.
   291	5. **`bunker://` parser**: 13 LOC, fully testable. NMP's NIP-46 module should ship this as `IdentityDescriptor::parse_bunker_uri(s: &str) -> Result<BunkerUri, IdentityError>`.
   292	6. **No "active marker" in serialization is an option**, but NDK's choice (persist activePubkey) better fits NMP's "session resumption" UX. Persist it.
   293	7. **`metadata$` per-account state** belongs in NMP's domain stores, not the IdentityModule. Don't pollute `Descriptor`.
   294	8. **NostrConnect's `repeat() + retry()` for reconnect** maps to NMP's relay pool — the NIP-46 client must be a *consumer* of the pool, not a peer of it.
   295	9. **`switchRelays` NIP-46 extension** — implement from M6. 5s timeout is the right default.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/research/sessions/ndk-sessions.md | sed -n '1,300p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# NDK Sessions + Signers — Deep Dive
     2	
     3	> Source: `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/NDK-nhlteu` (master). Read for NMP M6 to learn what the most-deployed JS Nostr library actually does about multi-account, signer plumbing, persistence boundaries, and reactive switching.
     4	
     5	## 1. Package layout
     6	
     7	NDK's session story lives in **three** packages, not one:
     8	
     9	- `sessions/src/*` (1.7k LOC) — pure session/signer state machine with pluggable storage. Built on `zustand/vanilla` (`store.ts:11`).
    10	- `core/src/signers/*` — the `NDKSigner` trait + registry + four built-in signers (PrivateKey, NIP-07, NIP-46 client+backend); `mobile/src/signers/nip55.ts` adds the Android Amber bridge by calling `registerSigner("nip55", …)`.
    11	- `react/src/session/*` — a *parallel* zustand store that pre-dates the new `sessions` package and is still the React integration layer (`react/src/session/store/types.ts:16`). Diverges in field names (`NDKUserSession` vs `NDKSession`) and embeds profile resolution.
    12	
    13	**Applies to NMP:** the kernel must do the work of NDK's `sessions` package internally — there will be no separate per-frontend session store. Native shadows derive from `AppState` per D4.
    14	
    15	**Diverges from NMP:** zustand-vanilla as the reactivity primitive is a JS-only choice; NMP's equivalent is the actor + `AppUpdate::FullState`/`ViewBatch` pipeline (`docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:170`).
    16	
    17	## 2. The `NDKSession` shape
    18	
    19	`sessions/src/types.ts:7-51` — what NDK keeps **per active account** (in memory):
    20	
    21	```ts
    22	export interface NDKSession {
    23	    pubkey: Hexpubkey;
    24	    followSet?: Set<Hexpubkey>;        // from kind:3
    25	    muteSet?: Map<string, string>;     // from kind:10000 ("p"|"e" → id)
    26	    mutedWords?: Set<string>;          // from kind:10000 "word" tags
    27	    blockedRelays?: Set<string>;       // from kind:10001
    28	    relayList?: Map<string, { read: boolean; write: boolean }>; // kind:10002
    29	    events: Map<NDKKind, NDKEvent | null>;   // replaceable-event cache
    30	    subscriptions: NDKSubscription[];        // live REQs owned by this session
    31	    lastActive: number;
    32	    preferences?: SessionPreferences;        // app-level (only walletEnabled today)
    33	}
    34	```
    35	
    36	What gets **persisted** is much smaller (`sessions/src/types.ts:135-140`):
    37	
    38	```ts
    39	export interface SerializedSession {
    40	    pubkey: Hexpubkey;
    41	    signerPayload?: string;   // result of NDKSigner.toPayload()
    42	    lastActive: number;
    43	    preferences?: SessionPreferences;
    44	}
    45	```
    46	
    47	Comment on the serializer (`sessions/src/persistence-manager.ts:65-67`): *"Only restores identity and signer — data will be fetched from relays/cache."*
    48	
    49	**Applies to NMP:** the persistence boundary is principled — persist *identity + signer descriptor + user preferences*; everything else is derived from the event store at next start. This maps cleanly onto NMP's actor-owned domain stores: the kernel persists `AccountId` + `IdentityDescriptor` blob; follow set, mute set, relay list are derived from the verified event store.
    50	
    51	## 3. `NDKSessionManager` — the public API
    52	
    53	`sessions/src/manager.ts:54-478`. Public surface that NMP's `IdentityModule` API should compare against:
    54	
    55	- `login(userOrSigner, { setActive })` (line 147) — accepts a signer OR a bare user (read-only account). Returns the pubkey.
    56	- `createAccount({ profile, relays, wallet, follows }, { publish, signer })` (line 193) — generates an nsec, optionally publishes kind:0/10002/3 + sets up NIP-60 wallet.
    57	- `switchTo(pubkey | null)` (line 280) — null deactivates.
    58	- `logout(pubkey?)` (line 273) — defaults to active.
    59	- `startSession(pubkey, opts: SessionStartOptions)` (line 287) / `stopSession` (line 294) — control what kinds get subscribed for this pubkey.
    60	- `addMonitor([NDKEventConstructor | NDKKind, ...])` (line 307) — incremental kind-monitor extension on the active session.
    61	- `enableWallet(pubkey?)` / `disableWallet` / `isWalletEnabled` (lines 322–394) — *opt-in* fetching for NIP-60 wallet (kind:17375 etc.). Crucially, this **restarts the subscription** so wallet kinds are not over-fetched for accounts that won't use them.
    62	- `restore()` (line 413) — load + start subscriptions for restored sessions; honors per-session `walletEnabled` preference.
    63	- `subscribe(cb)` (line 406) — pass-through to zustand subscribe.
    64	
    65	**Applies to NMP:** *opt-in subscription per concern* is right. NMP should keep this discipline (follows, mutes, relay list, wallet are separate fetch toggles). Maps to NMP's `ViewSpec` discipline (D5 snapshots bounded by what's open).
    66	
    67	**Applies to NMP:** `createAccount` packages a "generate + publish kind:0/10002/3 + opt-in wallet" flow into one call — matches M6's "Create new nsec flow" deliverable in `docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:14`.
    68	
    69	**Diverges from NMP:** NDK's manager owns NDK-instance side effects directly (`ndk.signer = …`, `ndk.muteFilter = …`). NMP's signer is selected by `ActivateAccount { pubkey }` (`docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:108`) and bound through the actor; no public assignment of a global "current signer" exists.
    70	
    71	## 4. The store layer — what reactivity buys you
    72	
    73	`sessions/src/store.ts` is the heart. Three patterns worth importing into NMP's mental model:
    74	
    75	### 4.1 Switch order matters (`store.ts:254-329`)
    76	
    77	```ts
    78	switchToUser: async (pubkey: Hexpubkey | null) => {
    79	    // ...
    80	    // Update NDK signer BEFORE setting activePubkey
    81	    // This prevents race conditions where reactive code runs before signer is ready
    82	    if (state.ndk) {
    83	        state.ndk.signer = signer;
    84	        // ... muteFilter / relayConnectionFilter rewire ...
    85	    }
    86	    set({ activePubkey: pubkey });
    87	    // ...
    88	    if (state.ndk && signer) {
    89	        const user = await signer.user();
    90	        user.ndk = state.ndk;
    91	        state.ndk.activeUser = user;
    92	    }
    93	}
    94	```
    95	
    96	The signer is wired *before* `activePubkey` flips so any zustand subscriber waking from the activePubkey change sees a fully-configured NDK. **Applies to NMP:** the actor's `ActivateAccount` handler must publish a single `AppUpdate::FullState` after *all* derived state is rebuilt — partial-update visibility is a class of bug NDK hit (the comment is "//prevents race conditions").
    97	
    98	### 4.2 The mute/relay filter is rewired on switch, not stored
    99	
   100	`store.ts:286-316` — `ndk.muteFilter` becomes a closure over the *session's* mute data; `ndk.relayConnectionFilter` becomes a closure over `blockedRelays`. Both are cleared on switch-to-null. **Applies to NMP:** the kernel's per-account filter sets must be rebound atomically with `ActivateAccount`; nothing outside the actor should hold a "current mute filter" reference.
   101	
   102	### 4.3 kind:3 / kind:10002 / kind:10000 auto-rewire is integral
   103	
   104	`store.ts:417-444` builds the subscription kinds list mechanically from `SessionStartOptions`:
   105	
   106	```ts
   107	function buildSubscriptionKinds(opts: SessionStartOptions, monitorKinds: NDKKind[]): NDKKind[] {
   108	    const kinds: NDKKind[] = [];
   109	    if (opts.follows) kinds.push(NDKKind.Contacts);
   110	    if (opts.mutes) kinds.push(NDKKind.MuteList);
   111	    if (opts.blockedRelays) kinds.push(NDKKind.BlockRelayList);
   112	    if (opts.relayList) kinds.push(NDKKind.RelayList);
   113	    if (opts.wallet) kinds.push(NDKKind.CashuWallet, NDKKind.CashuMintList);
   114	    kinds.push(...monitorKinds);
   115	    return kinds;
   116	}
   117	```
   118	
   119	Then `handleIncomingEvent` (`store.ts:449-487`) dispatches per-kind to `handleContactListEvent` / `handleMuteListEvent` / `handleBlockRelayListEvent` / `handleRelayListEvent`. Each handler:
   120	1. Loads the existing event for that kind (`session.events.get(event.kind)`).
   121	2. Drops events with same id or older `created_at` (`store.ts:498-499`, repeated in every handler).
   122	3. Rebuilds the derived set (`followSet` / `muteSet` / `mutedWords` / `relayList`) **and** stores the raw event for later inspection.
   123	
   124	**Applies to NMP:** the kernel's account-data planner should similarly drive a fixed subscription (kind:3 + kind:10002 + kind:10000 + kind:10001 for the active account) and rebuild derived domain rows on every newer-`created_at` arrival. This is mechanically the right way to satisfy D4 (single writer per derived fact).
   125	
   126	**Diverges from NMP:** NDK puts both the raw event *and* derived sets on the same session struct. NMP would put the raw event in the verified event store and the derived `FollowSet` / `MuteSet` in their own domain stores per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:445`. Same data, different home.
   127	
   128	## 5. The signer trait (`core/src/signers/index.ts:18-84`)
   129	
   130	```ts
   131	export interface NDKSigner {
   132	    get pubkey(): string;                                     // sync; throws "Not ready"
   133	    blockUntilReady(): Promise<NDKUser>;                      // initializer
   134	    user(): Promise<NDKUser>;
   135	    get userSync(): NDKUser;                                  // throws if not ready
   136	    sign(event: NostrEvent): Promise<string>;                 // returns sig only
   137	    relays?(ndk?: NDK): Promise<NDKRelay[]>;                  // optional NIP-65 hint
   138	    encryptionEnabled?(scheme?: NDKEncryptionScheme): Promise<NDKEncryptionScheme[]>;
   139	    encrypt(recipient: NDKUser, value: string, scheme?): Promise<string>;
   140	    decrypt(sender: NDKUser, value: string, scheme?): Promise<string>;
   141	    toPayload(): string;                                      // type-tagged JSON
   142	}
   143	
   144	export interface NDKSignerStatic<T extends NDKSigner> {
   145	    fromPayload(payload: string, ndk?: NDK): Promise<T>;
   146	}
   147	```
   148	
   149	Notable choices:
   150	- `pubkey` getter is **sync** but can throw `"Not ready"` for NIP-07/NIP-46 — caller must call `blockUntilReady()` first. Documented at `index.ts:21-23`.
   151	- `sign` returns only the signature string, not a signed event. The caller assembles.
   152	- `encryption*` are required on the interface but split by scheme (`"nip04" | "nip44"`); querying via `encryptionEnabled(scheme?)` returns the supported subset.
   153	- Serialization is via two methods: `toPayload()` returns `{type, payload}` JSON; the registry (`registry.ts:7-14`) dispatches to `static fromPayload`. Adding a new signer type means calling `registerSigner("name", Class)` at module import (e.g. `private-key/index.ts:241`, `nip07/index.ts:302`, `nip46/index.ts:613`, `mobile/src/signers/nip55.ts:185`).
   154	
   155	**Applies to NMP:** the **type-tagged registry** for signer serialization is the right pattern. NMP already has `IdentityModule::Descriptor` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/identity.rs:11`) which is the moral equivalent. Make sure the registry is in `nmp-core` so extension modules (e.g. an NIP-46 module) can register without core knowing the type.
   156	
   157	**Applies to NMP:** errors-as-sentinels (`throw new Error("Not ready")`) is exactly what D6 forbids. NMP must return `Result<SignedEvent, SigningError>` (already does — `identity.rs:18-22, 72-76`).
   158	
   159	## 6. The four built-in signers
   160	
   161	### 6.1 `NDKPrivateKeySigner` (`core/src/signers/private-key/index.ts`, 242 LOC)
   162	
   163	Accepts hex *or* nsec via one ctor (line 43-58 — note the `nsec1`/length-64 sniff). Adds NIP-49 helpers `encryptToNcryptsec(password, logn, ksb)` (line 111) and `fromNcryptsec(ncryptsec, password)` (line 140). `toPayload` (line 211) stores the hex key in plaintext — **explicit choice not to wrap in NIP-49 at the persistence layer**; that's the consumer's responsibility.
   164	
   165	**Applies to NMP:** the `KeychainCapability` from `docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:198-203` is exactly the layer NDK leaves to the consumer. The kernel should NIP-49-encrypt nsecs before handing them to `KeychainCapability::store`, since `KeychainCapability` only sees opaque bytes. (`docs/plan/m6-signers-write.md:14-15` agrees: "Create new nsec flow: generate, encrypt (NIP-49), and store via `KeychainCapability`.")
   166	
   167	### 6.2 `NDKNip07Signer` (`core/src/signers/nip07/index.ts`, 303 LOC)
   168	
   169	Wraps `window.nostr`. Three things to copy:
   170	- `waitForExtension()` (line 222-246) polls with `setInterval` + bounded `waitTimeout`. Returns rejection if extension never appears.
   171	- **Encryption queue** (`encryptionQueue` line 34, `processEncryptionQueue` line 169) — serializes encrypt/decrypt calls and retries on `"call already executing"` with backoff. Without this, two concurrent encrypts to a NIP-07 extension race.
   172	- `toPayload` (line 253) stores only `{"type":"nip07","payload":""}` — there's nothing to persist; on restore a fresh `NDKNip07Signer` is constructed and re-prompts.
   173	
   174	**Applies to NMP:** on web (post-M15), the NMP wasm shim needs the same queue behavior. The wasm/JS bridge runs in the same single-threaded event loop, so a queue at the JS bridge layer (analogous to applesauce's `BaseAccount.operation()`) handles the same race.
   175	
   176	### 6.3 `NDKNip46Signer` (`core/src/signers/nip46/index.ts`, 614 LOC)
   177	
   178	The biggest. Three constructor flows distinguished by string-shape (`index.ts:149-156`):
   179	
   180	```ts
   181	if (userOrConnectionToken === false) { /* deserialization path */ }
   182	else if (!userOrConnectionToken) { this.nostrconnectFlowInit(nostrConnectOptions); }
   183	else if (userOrConnectionToken.startsWith("bunker://")) { this.bunkerFlowInit(userOrConnectionToken); }
   184	else { this.nip05Init(userOrConnectionToken); }
   185	```
   186	
   187	The `bunker://` parser (`index.ts:204-215`):
   188	
   189	```ts
   190	private bunkerFlowInit(connectionToken: string) {
   191	    const bunkerUrl = new URL(connectionToken);
   192	    const bunkerPubkey = bunkerUrl.hostname || bunkerUrl.pathname.replace(/^\/\//, "");
   193	    const userPubkey = bunkerUrl.searchParams.get("pubkey");
   194	    const relayUrls = bunkerUrl.searchParams.getAll("relay");
   195	    const secret = bunkerUrl.searchParams.get("secret");
   196	    this.bunkerPubkey = bunkerPubkey; this.userPubkey = userPubkey;
   197	    this.relayUrls = relayUrls; this.secret = secret;
   198	}
   199	```
   200	
   201	Note: `hostname || pathname.replace(/^\/\//, "")` to defend against Firefox vs Chrome differences in WHATWG URL parsing (also explicitly called out in applesauce — see `applesauce-sessions.md` §6). The `secret` parameter is forwarded to the bunker's `connect` RPC (`index.ts:347-351`) — it is the bunker's challenge-response handshake.
   202	
   203	`blockUntilReady` for the bunker flow (`index.ts:315-368`): subscribes on `localPubkey`'s NIP-46 RPC topic, sends a `connect` request with `[userPubkey, secret?]`, awaits `"ack"`, then issues `get_public_key`, then `switchRelays`. The `authUrl` event (line 342-344) lets the application open a popup for additional auth.
   204	
   205	`switchRelays` (`index.ts:375-411`): a NIP-46 protocol extension where the bunker can tell the client which relays to migrate to. Times out at 5s if the bunker doesn't reply — important so old/simple bunkers don't hang the login.
   206	
   207	`toPayload` (`index.ts:536-553`) persists `bunkerPubkey + userPubkey + relayUrls + secret + localSignerPayload`. `fromPayload` (`index.ts:562-610`) re-subscribes and is ready to sign — **does not re-issue `connect`** because the bunker already trusts this `localSigner`. This is the "reconnect after app restart" path.
   208	
   209	**NDKNip46TimeoutError** (`index.ts:21-33`) — typed timeout error with the operation name. `signer.timeout` is opt-in (default undefined). **Applies to NMP:** the `IdentityError` enum in `identity.rs:50-53` needs a `Timeout(operation, ms)` variant (currently only has `InvalidDescriptor` and `Storage`).
   210	
   211	### 6.4 `NDKNip55Signer` (`mobile/src/signers/nip55.ts`, 186 LOC)
   212	
   213	Android Amber bridge via `expo-nip55`. Important shape: stores `packageName` + cached `_pubkey` in `toPayload` (line 139) so re-creating the signer doesn't re-prompt the user for app selection. **Applies to NMP:** the Apple Secure Enclave / iOS-passkey signer (post-M6) will have the same shape — store an opaque platform-side handle (`packageName` analog) plus cached pubkey; on restore, the platform-side capability uses that handle to re-establish the link.
   214	
   215	## 7. Storage adapters (`sessions/src/storage/*.ts`)
   216	
   217	Three implementations behind one interface (`storage/types.ts:8-23`):
   218	
   219	```ts
   220	export interface SessionStorage {
   221	    save(sessions: Map<Hexpubkey, SerializedSession>, activePubkey?: Hexpubkey): Promise<void>;
   222	    load(): Promise<SessionData>;
   223	    clear(): Promise<void>;
   224	}
   225	```
   226	
   227	`LocalStorage` (`local-storage.ts`, 59 LOC) — `localStorage[key] = JSON.stringify(...)`. Throws `StorageError("localStorage is not available")` outside browsers (line 14-17). `FileStorage` (`file-storage.ts`, 56 LOC) — dynamic-imports `fs/promises` so it tree-shakes out of browser bundles. `MemoryStorage` for tests. **No platform-native (Keychain/Keystore) storage** — those are explicitly the consumer's job, same boundary as NMP's `KeyringCapability`.
   228	
   229	`PersistenceManager` (`persistence-manager.ts`) is the orchestration layer. Save (line 43-51) serializes all sessions, writes. Restore (line 23-39) loads, recreates sessions WITHOUT activating, then activates the saved active pubkey. **Important:** `restoreSession` (line 68-95) tolerates a missing signer payload — the session re-appears as read-only if the signer fails to deserialize (e.g. NIP-07 extension uninstalled, NIP-46 bunker pubkey rotated). This is how a "lost signer" downgrades gracefully to npub-only mode rather than crashing the session.
   230	
   231	**Applies to NMP:** The kernel must do the same — a startup-time signer-resurrection failure must downgrade to read-only and surface a toast (per D6), not break the session entirely.
   232	
   233	## 8. Errors (`sessions/src/utils/errors.ts`)
   234	
   235	Six typed errors all extending `SessionError extends Error`. The discriminator is `error.name` (set in each constructor). `SignerDeserializationError`, `StorageError`, `SessionNotFoundError`, `NoActiveSessionError`, `NDKNotInitializedError`. **Applies to NMP:** NMP's `IdentityError` enum (`identity.rs:50-53`) currently has only two variants; the equivalent set is needed when M6 lands — at minimum `NotFound(pubkey)`, `NotActive`, `SignerRestoreFailed(reason)`, `KeyringUnavailable`, `Timeout(op, ms)`.
   236	
   237	## 9. Auto-save (`manager.ts:467-477`)
   238	
   239	```ts
   240	private setupAutoSave(): void {
   241	    const debouncedPersist = debounce(() => {
   242	        this.persistenceManager.persist().catch((error) => {
   243	            console.error("Failed to auto-save sessions:", error);
   244	        });
   245	    }, this.options.saveDebounceMs!);
   246	    this.unsubscribe = this.store.subscribe(() => { debouncedPersist(); });
   247	}
   248	```
   249	
   250	Subscribes to zustand store, debounces a save by `saveDebounceMs` (default 500ms, `manager.ts:65`). Save errors are *logged*, not propagated — partial-failure isolation. **Applies to NMP:** session persistence in NMP should be similarly debounced (the actor can use a heartbeat tick rather than a JS debounce). Errors become toast state per D6, not bubbled to the platform.
   251	
   252	## 10. The React layer (`react/src/session/store/*`) — divergence note
   253	
   254	The react package has its **own** zustand store that pre-dates the `sessions` package. Key differences worth knowing because it's what the iOS app's mental model probably came from:
   255	
   256	- `NDKUserSession` (`react/src/session/store/types.ts:16-38`) carries `profile?: NDKUserProfile` directly on the session — the new `sessions/types.ts:7` does NOT (profile is its own concern).
   257	- `switchToUser` in react (`react/src/session/store/switch-to-user.ts:13-70`) couples with a separate `useNDKMutes` store and a `useNDKStore.getState().setSigner(signer)` — i.e., the signer lives in yet another zustand atom. This is the "monolithic Nostr client object quietly accumulating concerns" pattern NMP's lessons doc warns against (`docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:31`).
   258	
   259	**Diverges from NMP:** NMP must NOT replicate the multi-store split. Identity, mutes, signer are all owned by the actor and projected through `AppState`.
   260	
   261	## 11. NDK summary table — what to copy, what to skip
   262	
   263	| Pattern | Cite | NMP verdict |
   264	| --- | --- | --- |
   265	| Type-tagged signer registry | `core/src/signers/registry.ts:7-14` | **copy** — fits `IdentityModule::Descriptor` |
   266	| `toPayload`/`fromPayload` per signer | `nip07/index.ts:253-278`, `nip46/index.ts:536-610` | **copy** the principle; use serde+enum dispatch in Rust |
   267	| Persist identity+signer only; derive everything else from events | `persistence-manager.ts:65-67` | **copy** — matches D4 |
   268	| Per-session opt-in fetch toggles (`follows`, `mutes`, `wallet`, …) | `manager.ts:287, 322, 356` | **copy** — fits ViewSpec + D5 |
   269	| Switch order: signer before activePubkey, atomic publish | `store.ts:279-329` | **copy as actor invariant** |
   270	| Storage adapter interface w/ memory/file/local impls | `storage/types.ts:8-23` | **copy** — but NMP's adapter is LMDB + `KeyringCapability` |
   271	| `NDKNip46TimeoutError` typed timeout | `nip46/index.ts:21-33` | **copy** — add to `IdentityError` |
   272	| `restoreSession` tolerates missing signer → read-only | `persistence-manager.ts:75-88` | **copy** as actor behavior |
   273	| zustand-vanilla as primitive | `store.ts:11, 98` | **skip** — NMP uses actor + `AppUpdate` |
   274	| Global `ndk.signer = …` mutation | `store.ts:282` | **skip** — NMP uses `ActivateAccount` intent |
   275	| Direct `ndk.muteFilter = (event) => …` closure | `store.ts:286-302` | **skip** — NMP filters live in domain stores |
   276	| Profile on session struct | `react/src/session/store/types.ts:18` | **skip** — profile is its own domain store |
   277	| React package's parallel store | `react/src/session/store/*` | **anti-pattern** — never repeat |

     1	import type { Hexpubkey } from "@nostr-dev-kit/ndk";
     2	import { NDKUser as NDKUserClass } from "@nostr-dev-kit/ndk";
     3	import { serializeSession } from "./serialization/session";
     4	import { deserializeSigner } from "./serialization/signer";
     5	import type { SessionStorage } from "./storage/types";
     6	import type { SessionStore } from "./store";
     7	import type { SerializedSession } from "./types";
     8	import { SignerDeserializationError, StorageError } from "./utils/errors";
     9	
    10	/**
    11	 * Manages session persistence and restoration.
    12	 * Handles saving, loading, and clearing sessions from storage.
    13	 */
    14	export class PersistenceManager {
    15	    constructor(
    16	        private storage: SessionStorage | undefined,
    17	        private getStore: () => SessionStore,
    18	    ) {}
    19	
    20	    /**
    21	     * Restore sessions from storage
    22	     */
    23	    async restore(): Promise<void> {
    24	        if (!this.storage) {
    25	            throw new StorageError("No storage configured");
    26	        }
    27	
    28	        const { sessions: serializedSessions, activePubkey } = await this.storage.load();
    29	
    30	        // Restore each session
    31	        for (const [pubkey, serialized] of serializedSessions) {
    32	            await this.restoreSession(pubkey, serialized);
    33	        }
    34	
    35	        // Restore active session
    36	        if (activePubkey && this.getStore().sessions.has(activePubkey)) {
    37	            this.getStore().switchToUser(activePubkey);
    38	        }
    39	    }
    40	
    41	    /**
    42	     * Persist all sessions to storage
    43	     */
    44	    async persist(): Promise<void> {
    45	        if (!this.storage) {
    46	            throw new StorageError("No storage configured");
    47	        }
    48	
    49	        const serialized = await this.serializeAllSessions();
    50	        await this.storage.save(serialized, this.getStore().activePubkey);
    51	    }
    52	
    53	    /**
    54	     * Clear all sessions from storage
    55	     */
    56	    async clear(): Promise<void> {
    57	        if (!this.storage) {
    58	            throw new StorageError("No storage configured");
    59	        }
    60	
    61	        await this.storage.clear();
    62	    }
    63	
    64	    /**
    65	     * Restore a single session from serialized data
    66	     * Only restores identity and signer - data will be fetched from relays/cache
    67	     */
    68	    private async restoreSession(pubkey: Hexpubkey, serialized: SerializedSession): Promise<void> {
    69	        const store = this.getStore();
    70	
    71	        // Attempt to restore signer
    72	        let signer;
    73	        if (serialized.signerPayload) {
    74	            try {
    75	                signer = await deserializeSigner(serialized.signerPayload, store.ndk);
    76	            } catch (error) {
    77	                // Log error but continue - session can work without signer
    78	                if (error instanceof SignerDeserializationError) {
    79	                    console.warn(`Failed to restore signer for ${pubkey}: ${error.message}`);
    80	                }
    81	            }
    82	        }
    83	
    84	        // Create user (with or without signer)
    85	        const user = signer ? await signer.user() : (store.ndk?.getUser({ pubkey }) ?? new NDKUserClass({ pubkey }));
    86	
    87	        // Add session without activating
    88	        await store.addSession(signer || user, false);
    89	
    90	        // Restore lastActive timestamp and preferences
    91	        store.updateSession(pubkey, {
    92	            lastActive: serialized.lastActive,
    93	            preferences: serialized.preferences,
    94	        });
    95	    }
    96	
    97	    /**
    98	     * Serialize all sessions for storage
    99	     */
   100	    private async serializeAllSessions(): Promise<Map<Hexpubkey, SerializedSession>> {
   101	        const store = this.getStore();
   102	        const serialized = new Map<Hexpubkey, SerializedSession>();
   103	
   104	        for (const [pubkey, session] of store.sessions) {
   105	            const signer = store.signers.get(pubkey);
   106	            const serializedSession = await serializeSession(session, signer);
   107	            serialized.set(pubkey, serializedSession);
   108	        }
   109	
   110	        return serialized;
   111	    }
   112	}

     1	import type { Hexpubkey } from "@nostr-dev-kit/ndk";
     2	import type { SerializedSession } from "../types";
     3	import { StorageError } from "../utils/errors";
     4	import { deserializeSessionData, type SessionData, serializeSessionData } from "../utils/json-serializer";
     5	import type { SessionStorage } from "./types";
     6	
     7	/**
     8	 * Browser localStorage implementation
     9	 */
    10	export class LocalStorage implements SessionStorage {
    11	    constructor(private key: string = "ndk-sessions") {}
    12	
    13	    async save(sessions: Map<Hexpubkey, SerializedSession>, activePubkey?: Hexpubkey): Promise<void> {
    14	        if (typeof window === "undefined" || !window.localStorage) {
    15	            throw new StorageError("localStorage is not available");
    16	        }
    17	
    18	        try {
    19	            const data: SessionData = { sessions, activePubkey };
    20	            const serialized = serializeSessionData(data);
    21	            localStorage.setItem(this.key, serialized);
    22	        } catch (error) {
    23	            const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error";
    24	            throw new StorageError(`Failed to save sessions to localStorage: ${message}`);
    25	        }
    26	    }
    27	
    28	    async load(): Promise<SessionData> {
    29	        if (typeof window === "undefined" || !window.localStorage) {
    30	            return { sessions: new Map() };
    31	        }
    32	
    33	        const raw = localStorage.getItem(this.key);
    34	        if (!raw) {
    35	            return { sessions: new Map() };
    36	        }
    37	
    38	        try {
    39	            return deserializeSessionData(raw);
    40	        } catch (error) {
    41	            const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error";
    42	            throw new StorageError(`Failed to parse sessions from localStorage: ${message}`);
    43	        }
    44	    }
    45	
    46	    async clear(): Promise<void> {
    47	        if (typeof window === "undefined" || !window.localStorage) {
    48	            throw new StorageError("localStorage is not available");
    49	        }
    50	
    51	        try {
    52	            localStorage.removeItem(this.key);
    53	        } catch (error) {
    54	            const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error";
    55	            throw new StorageError(`Failed to clear sessions from localStorage: ${message}`);
    56	        }
    57	    }
    58	}
